As part of the DevOps group you will work on products critical to our operations and software that proactively detects issues with systems in the data center, in the cloud, or at customer sites. Responsibilities DevOps Engineers will have a variety of responsibilities which may include things like: * Design and implement pipelines and tools to ensure continuous integration, delivery, and deployment for our products * Define and implement tools that enable other teams to follow DevOps best practices * Own and deliver a suite of platform services consumed by internal stakeholders across teams and functions * Work on cloud environments ensuring reliability and availability * Ensure production deployments are fast, reliable, and safe * Work with geo\-distributed development teams to improve quality and time\-to deploy * Automate, Automate, Automate Qualifications * Required * Relevant Bachelor's degree (Computer Science, Engineering, etc.) * 1\-2 years hands\-on DevOps/SRE experience * Belief in DevOps and Agile principles * Zealous about automation * Comfort in dealing with Cloud deployments, networking, and security best practices * CI/CD pipeline experience (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, etc.) * Hands on experience on Amazon Web Services (AWS) * Hands on experience with Kubernetes * Linux system proficiency Preferred * Strong container/serverless experience * Proficient knowledge of Python language * Experience on Hashicorp ecosystem \- Terraform, Packer, Consul, Vault * Experience with telemetry tools/pipelines (metrics, logs, and traces) * Experience with RDS databases

title: DevOps Engineer
description:  Design, build, and enhance CI/CD pipelines that enable fast, reliable deployment of microservices across multiple regions.  Support and evolve multi\-tenant and multi\-region deployment models as we transition to a modern environment strategy.  Build and maintain Kubernetes\-based platforms, delivering secure, scalable,and developer\-friendly environments.  Implement Infrastructure\-as\-Code using Terraform and Helm to standardise and automate infrastructure provisioning.  Strengthen observability, monitoring, and alerting to proactively identify issues and support high\-quality delivery.  Improve developer experience through automation, self\-service tooling, reusable patterns, and scalable pipelines.  Embed security and compliance into delivery workflows, ensuring strong auditability and policy adherence.  Collaborate with engineering to troubleshoot and optimise deployment processes and runtime environments.  Champion modern DevOps and platform engineering practices throughout the engineering organisation. What you will need to have  Proven experience in DevOps or platform engineering roles supporting distributed, cloud\-based systems.  Strong hands\-on experience with AWS cloud services and cloud\-native architectures.  Experience with Kubernetes, container orchestration, and service deployment patterns.  Expertise in Terraform and Helm, with a strong automation mindset.  Hands\-on experience building and operating CI/CD pipelines — ideally Jenkins.  Strong Docker, containerisation, and automation fundamentals.  Familiarity with modern observability tooling and best practices.  A focus on creating scalable, repeatable, and secure delivery workflows.  Passion for enabling development teams and improving tooling, velocity, and reliability. Nice to have  Experience working in financial services or other regulated environments.  Exposure to multi\-region and multi\-tenant platform strategies.  Experience supporting systems in an event\-driven environment (Kafka familiarity).  Background in legacy\-to\-modern migration initiatives.  Familiarity with GitOps tooling or patterns.  Knowledge of compliance automation and security policy enforcement in pipelines."

title: Engine Field Service Technician

title: External Supply – Manufacturing Support - Manufacturing Process Owner
description: Ireland \- Dun Laoghaire JOB ID: R\-243553 LOCATION: Ireland \- Dun Laoghaire WORK LOCATION TYPE: Flex Commuter / Hybrid DATE POSTED: Apr. 27, 2026 CATEGORY: Manufacturing **External Supply – Manufacturing Support \- Manufacturing Process Owner** **Job Summary:** The External Supply \- Manufacturing Support Process Owner role is a key role in the newly formed Manufacturing support team requiring in\-depth technical and operational knowledge of key capabilities in our external network. The Manufacturing Support team will include a variety of roles and will report to the Associate Director of Manufacturing Support. We are currently hiring two of these roles as follows: Role 1: Aseptic DP processes; Formulation, Filling and Lyophilisation Role 2: Capping, visual inspection and packaging These roles will support key areas of the business; the Manufacturing Process Owner will direct initiatives that deliver execution excellence in our CDMOs, improve the reliability and efficiency of manufacturing processes in addition to day\-to\-day support related to unplanned event troubleshooting, investigations, CAPA identification and implementation. **Primary responsibilities:** * Provide day\-to\-day operational oversight to ensure execution excellence via successful batch execution and on\-time delivery of product. * Lead and or support manufacturing / ops readiness * Supporting manufacturing in delivering supply to patients through Ownership of the assigned processes and taking a proactive approach to safety, quality and compliance by actively seek opportunities to remove error traps. * Subject Matter Expert with thorough in\-depth knowledge of the assigned processes and interfaces, ensuring audit and inspection readiness. * Monitor campaign progress and production schedules, identifying and addressing risks. * Track operational performance such as yield, cycle time, and right\-first\-time metrics. * Accountable point of contact for knowledge of CDMO process documentation (eg SOPs, Training, Electronic Batch Records, Risk Assessments) in assigned area of responsibility. Gatekeeper (develop, review and update) of changes to CDMO process documentation to maintain structure and control over generation and revision ensuring the documents clearly and correctly direct the process. * Lead and/or Support investigations that impact on safety, quality and/or compliance of the processes, ensuring thorough investigations are carried out and actionable CAPAs, to prevent future reoccurrence, are identified and implemented within agreed timelines. * Ownership and project management, within agreed timelines, of CAPAs and Change Controls at our CDMOs. * Utilises subject matter expertise to identify and lead continuous improvement projects in assigned area of responsibility. Ensuring project timelines are met, challenges identified, mitigations are in place and communication plans delivered to all stakeholders. * Engaging with staff members to ensure understanding of the processes and working collaboratively cross functionally with Manufacturing, Quality, PD and Supply Chain internally and with CDMOs. * Involved in the daily business management systems and reporting, having a clear understanding of what is happening in the assigned area of responsibility. Monitor any process trends, including business performance metrics. Identify actions that will enable the process and the CDMO’s Manufacturing areas better achieve and surpass metrics in the future. * Provide technical input to the shift teams on challenges that are encountered. Actively looking at ways to assist in the running of the business and provide support to actions that are related to assigned area of responsibility. * Providing expertise to Project Teams responsible for the introduction of new products and projects, ensuring successful implementation in the CDMO’s manufacturing area. * Collaborate with process and analytical teams to ensure accurate transfer of parameters, process understanding, and control strategies. * Effectively engaging and participating within a Lean culture environment where continuous improvement behaviours are expected, and programs are employed. The role will require incorporation of Lean concepts into assigned project(s). Managing and implementing projects that develop lean practices in the manufacturing areas. **Competencies/Skills:** * Ability to travel to sites in our CDMO network and ability to work successfully in a highly international environment * Problem solving skills: Gathers data from key stakeholders, analyses and interprets information to develop solutions to technical problems of moderate complexity * Project Management and organizational skills, including ability to follow assignments through to completion. * Demonstrated ability to work independently and deliver right first\-time results under minimal direction. * Escalate issues professionally and in a timely manner. * Negotiation and Influencing skills. * Take initiative to identify and drive improvements using lean six sigma tools. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills: Strong Technical writing skills for cGxP documentation (eg investigations, procedures, change controls) and good Presentation skills. * Decision Making skills and confidence to challenge others on decisions that impact the manufacturing area. * Teamwork and ability to coach, mentor and/or cross train colleagues within core technical areas. * Ensures compliance within regulatory environment. * Familiar with data analytics. **Education and Relevant Experience:** * Minimally Bachelor of Science, Engineering or technical discipline. * 5\+ years of manufacturing experience in relevant Drug Substance, Drug Product Manufacturing or related cGxP manufacturing experience. * Experience with regulatory compliance in cGxP manufacturing and in depth knowledge of Eudralex Volume 4, Annex 1 Manufacture of Sterile Medicinal Products.

title: FP&A Analyst
description: **We are partnering with a major national energy organisation to appoint an FP\&A Analyst. This is a key business‑facing role that blends financial modelling, forecasting, performance analysis, and cross‑functional stakeholder engagement.** **The position offers strong exposure to senior management, ownership of budgeting and planning cycles, and the opportunity to influence decision‑making through high‑quality financial insight and commentary.** **Responsibilities:** * Leading the budgeting, forecasting, and long‑term planning processes, ensuring timely and accurate submissions. * Partnering with business units to understand financial drivers, identify risks and opportunities, and support commercial decision‑making. * Analysing monthly performance, preparing clear variance analysis, insights, and presentations for senior stakeholders. * Developing and enhancing financial models, dashboards, and analytical tools to improve visibility and accuracy. * Driving continuous improvement and supporting automation across FP\&A processes. * Ensuring strong governance, control compliance, and alignment with audit standards. **Requirements:** * Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) with at least 2 years’ post‑qualification experience. * Excellent financial, analytical, and problem‑solving skills. * Strong Excel capability with experience in data‑driven analysis; Power BI or similar tools an advantage. * Confident communicator with the ability to engage and challenge senior stakeholders effectively. * Highly organised, detail‑oriented, and comfortable managing competing priorities. * Experience with budgeting, forecasting, or financial modelling beneficial; familiarity with SAP or other planning systems a plus. **Our client offers a hybrid working environment (minimum two days on‑site), flexible working arrangements, and the opportunity to contribute to Ireland’s transition to a net‑zero energy system.** Apply directly via the link provided or contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Financial Analyst

As a key member of the Finance team, reporting to the Senior Finance Manager, you will play an important role in enhancing the processes required to deliver optimal financial control and reporting accuracy. This position is ideal for a newly or recently qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA) who has an interest in the intersection of finance and IT systems. This is a highly collaborative and cross\-functional role where you will interact with all key areas of the business. **Key Responsibilities:** * Drive and maintain monthly reporting to provide analysis against operational KPIs, from departmental level down to individual fee earners * Deliver detailed financial analysis to support business units and decision\-making * Collaborate closely with the Finance Management Team on presentations and reporting requirements * Liaise with Partners and colleagues to respond to general finance queries * Support and mentor trainees, providing guidance as required * Lead and/or contribute to finance\-related projects and initiatives * Assist with ad hoc analysis and reporting as needed * Present on KPI performance at individual fee earner level * Act as a key liaison with the internal IT team on the practice management system * Contribute to system enhancements, software implementations, and continuous process improvements **About You:** * ACA / ACCA qualified with a strong academic record * 1–2 years’ post\-qualification experience preferred, but not essential * Strong analytical, reconciliation, and problem\-solving skills * Excellent attention to detail and strong IT capabilities * Experience with systems or implementations is advantageous * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment * Proactive, organised, and capable of managing multiple priorities **Why Join Us:** Excellent salary and benefits package, with a hybrid working model and plenty of scope for further career development and progression internally. **How to Apply** To apply, please contact Richard Minchin on the Lex Consultancy team.

title: Founding Engineer
description: Gontrel is live in Dublin with 10,000 downloads and a team in place. Now we need a founding engineer to own what comes next. Gontrel is a food discovery app (imagine TikTok but for food). You open the app, you see a great video of a restaurant, with Gontrel there's no need to leave the app to look for menu's, booking availability, distance.....everything is in the one app. You find all the information (videos, restaurant information) you're looking for without ever having to leave the app We're a small team and we move fast. We ship twice a week. The person we bring in here will have a real say in how the product is built. What you'll be working on: The recommendation engine: Helping users find content they love, faster. Video infrastructure: Upload, processing, playback. It needs to feel smooth and it needs to work every time. Creator tools: Making it easy for restaurants and food creators to post content, grow an audience, and get rewarded for it. Discovery: Search, location, the stuff that connects the right person to the right place at the right moment. Stack: Node.js, AWS, Swift (iOS). Backend leaning but you'll touch a bit of everything. Who we're looking for: We are looking for someone who has shipped a consumer app before. Someone who knows what it feels like to build something, put it in front of real people, watch what happens, and go again. That whole cycle. If that's you, we want to hear from you straight away. If you haven't done consumer apps, that's okay too. We'd just want to see something else that shows us you're the kind of person who loves building and shipping things that actually get used. You'll really enjoy this if you love the early stages, like to ship things and go again, care about how things feel to use, and want to own something not just contribute to it. What's in it for you: Equity: You'll own a piece of the company. Compensation: Competitive, we'll talk numbers directly. Location: In person, Dublin office. How to apply: We keep it simple. To apply, email info@gontrel.com with the subject line TEST. And we'll send you the details. Already built something in Swift? Skip the test and just send us the app instead.

title: Full Stack Engineer

EXL harnesses the power of data, AI, and deep industry knowledge to transform businesses, including the world’s leading corporations in industries including insurance, healthcare, banking and financial services, media and retail, among others. EXL was founded in 1999 with the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity and respect. We are headquartered in New York and have more than 60,000 employees spanning six continents. For more information, visit www.exlservice.com. **Role Title:** Full Stack Developer **BU/Segment:** Insurance **Location:** Dublin, Republic of Ireland (Flexible hybrid working) **Employment Type:** Permanent **Summary of the role:** Developers in this team are expected to work independently and think like product engineers, not just coders. They collaborate with the Technical Architect and Product Owner to translate business and UX requirements into secure, scalable, production\-grade solutions for the EXL Premium Audit platform. They design and implement end\-to\-end features (backend, frontend, integrations), proactively suggest improvements, and help shape exceptional auditor and carrier user experiences. They also provide day\-to\-day technical guidance to junior developers and ensure high\-quality delivery. **As part of your duties, you will be responsible for:** * Work with the Technical Architect, Product Owner and BAs to understand business requirements and convert them into technical designs and implementation tasks. * Design and build secure, scalable backend services and APIs using .NET / Entity Framework and/or Python, including data access, integrations, and background processing. * Develop modern, responsive UIs using Angular or React, JavaScript/TypeScript, HTML/CSS, and Bootstrap, with strong attention to usability and workflow efficiency. * Integrate with AI services and components in partnership with the AI Architect (e.g., LLM\-based validations, document analysis, recommendations), including calling AI APIs and handling results in a robust way. * Ensure code quality through unit and integration tests (e.g., JUnit, Jasmine, Karma or equivalent), code reviews, and adherence to coding standards and security best practices. * Use source control and DevOps tools (Git, TFS) effectively, contributing to CI/CD pipelines and deployment readiness. * Troubleshoot and resolve production issues within agreed SLAs, including root cause analysis and preventive improvements. * Assist in customization and integration of third\-party products and platforms (e.g., Nexus, other carrier systems) for internal and client use. * Mentor and support junior developers on the squad, providing review, guidance, and examples of good engineering practices. * Collaborate closely with QA Automation and Performance teams to ensure features are testable, observable, and performant. **Qualifications and experience we consider to be essential for the role:** * Solid experience (typically 4–7 years) in enterprise application development, ideally on workflow\-heavy or B2B platforms. * Strong hands\-on skills in .NET / Entity Framework and/or Python, plus a modern frontend framework (Angular). * Experience with form.io or similar schema\-driven dynamic form frameworks (JSON\-based forms, conditional logic, reuseable components) is a strong plus. **Skills and Personal attributes we would like to have:** * Good understanding of how AI systems work and prior experience integrating AI/ML/LLM services via APIs or SDKs. * Comfort working with distributed teams (India / Ireland / US) and owning features end\-to\-end with minimal supervision. * Demonstrated ability to think critically, challenge assumptions, and propose simpler/better technical solutions that improve user experience and business value. **As part of a leading global Data and AI company, you can look forward to:** * A competitive salary with a generous bonus, private healthcare, life assurance at 4 x your annual salary, income protection insurance, and a rewarding pension. * At **EXL** , we are committed to providing our employees with the tools and resources they need to succeed and excel in their careers. Our company does not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, age, or disability status. * At **EXL** , we offer a flexible hybrid working model that allows employees to live a balanced, healthy lifestyle while strengthening our culture of collaboration. **To be considered for this role, you must already be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland.**

title: Full Stack Engineer
description: **Job Title:** Senior Full Stack Engineer **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Type:** Hybrid **Model:** Contract **Duration:** 12 Months **Senior Full Stack Engineer \- WI Digital Platforms** Our client’s workplace encourages fresh ideas, creative thinking, and rewards forward\-thinking, passionate individuals. They pride themselves on fostering a diverse culture where individuals are empowered to share, collaborate, and be themselves within a highly dynamic and inclusive environment. Workplace Investing (WI) supports employers in delivering successful benefits programs designed to enhance their employees’ overall well\-being. If our client thrives in an Agile team environment and is passionate about using technology to solve business challenges, then the WI Digital Platforms team in Dublin could be the ideal next step in their career. **The Expertise You Have** • Bachelor’s or post\-primary degree in a Technology\-related subject area. • 5 or more years’ development experience with 3\+ in front\-end development. • Experience in building full stack web solutions. • Passion for delivering the best end user journeys. • Expertise in delivering front\-end solutions and mid\-tier services. **The Skills You Bring** • Expertise in building responsive web applications. • Strong experience of developing in frameworks such as Angular or React, or any other equivalent UI framework, e.g., NodeJS and NPM. • Strong knowledge with web fundamentals like HTML5, CSS and JavaScript. • Strong knowledge of Java, Spring, RESTful services, JUnit. • Proficient with release management, source control and CI/CD technologies, e.g. Jenkins, Git, Maven, etc. • Ability to apply previous experience of Automation Tools/Test Frameworks (e.g., Jasmine, Mocha, Selenium, Protractor, etc.). • Experience with any of the following a plus: Artifactory, Monorepo, Apache Camel, AWS / ECS / EKS, code analysis tools, e.g. Veracode, SOASTA, Sonar, JaCoCo, etc. **The Value You Deliver** • Collaborative working relationships with the Product Area as part of a global Agile team of architects, analysts and developers using Scrum. • Providing technical expertise in the UI, application, and service tiers. • Leading and guiding junior team members. • Delivering high quality, timely, cost effective and maintainable software solutions in an Agile environment to meet functional and non\-functional business requirements. • Giving your expertise to further Product Area and Technology capabilities outside the squad. title: Graduate Environmental Consultant
description: **Overview** **Shape a sustainable tomorrow.** Do you want to join a team that asks questions and dares to see things differently? We are AtkinsRéalis, a world\-class engineering services and nuclear organization. When you join us, you'll help create world\-class sustainable solutions that connect people, data, and technology. We're transforming infrastructure and energy systems, growing partnerships with our clients, and bringing the most complex projects across the globe to life. Join us and engineer a better future for our planet and its people. Our global team of 36,000\+ employees speak over 70 languages and represents 130 nationalities across six continents. Our work covers a variety of sectors, including transportation, infrastructure, environment, water, energy, defence, technology and aerospace. On our Graduate Development programme, you take the driver's seat in your career. Grow personally and professionally, guided by inspiring managers. Say yes to opportunities that spark your interest, knowing you'll have training and the support you need to maintain a healthy work\-life balance. Reaching your full potential means being able to bring your whole self to work and feeling like you belong. Find out more about our global programme Different makes a difference. **Where could you work** As a Graduate environmental engineer, you'll enjoy variety, working on large and small projects with the opportunity to explore different career pathways, including consulting and design. You'll be a part of our vibrant and expanding multidisciplinary team. We're the gateway to planning, urban design and Environmental Impact Assessment within the organisation, comprising a vibrant network of people providing world\-class consultancy and advisory services. We're committed to providing the best outcomes for our clients, always with sustainability in mind. We share our understanding of the unprecedented challenges they face – climate change, rapid urbanisation, biodiversity decline, consumerisation, economic uncertainty, security threats and technology\-driven change – to create a better future. The work we do is important and influential, it shapes the way our clients think, enables them to make better informed choices and deliver their projects effectively in a world of disruption and change. As a graduate, you'll have plenty of opportunities to be involved in a variety of projects, ranging from energy and renewables, water, nuclear, property, rail, highways, international projects, planning and stakeholder engagement. Your role * Supporting with the coordination of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) and other non\-statutory environmental appraisals. * Supporting with coordinating a multi\-disciplinary team of environmental specialists inputting to environmental assessments and environmental design inputs. * Collecting and analysing data including conducting field surveys where required. * Carrying out desk\-based baseline constraints studies and appraisals. * Writing factual reports and sharing findings with multi\-disciplinary colleagues, clients, sub\-contractors and/or regulators or other stakeholders. * Supporting Senior environmental engineers / consultants, as required, in the delivery of robust planning documentation, to required best practice standards, in accordance with an agreed scope of work, and to the agreed delivery programme. * Working with colleagues, clients, environmental specialists and engineering teams to help promote sustainable design solutions and environmental enhancement. **About You** * Minimum Level 8 degree in Earth Science, Environmental Assessment \& Management, Environmental Geoscience, Environmental Impact Assessment, Environmental Science, Environmental Studies, Geography or Sustainability. * A passion for working in a consultancy environment. * Championing a culture where everyone belongs. * A desire to work collaboratively with a curious approach, challenging yourself to find new solutions. * Enthusiasm and drive to develop your skillset and seek out development opportunities within a supportive team. * Motivation to achieve professional accreditation as your career progresses (where applicable). * Comfortably embrace agile ways of working and learning new technologies. * Commitment to spend a minimum of three days a week in the office or on client site (project dependent). Being together in person helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more effectively, and accelerate your learning and development. **Training and development** Our graduate development programme is designed to give you an insight into many areas of work, and the chance to explore where your own skills and interests lie. It features a series of training events and modules, professional development, mentoring and on\-the\-job experience as well as practical skills development. From day one, you'll work on real projects that offer scale and variety, collaborating with colleagues and creating meaningful impact on the world around us. You'll work towards your professional accreditation (where applicable) \- whichever path you take, your future starts here. We're proud to have been an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited employer since 2021\. This means you'll be supported by a true learning and development organization dedicated to your future. We can advise on the next deadline for applications (previous deadline was Sunday, 4th January 2026\). As our graduate development programmes are incredibly popular we recommend that you apply early as some roles may close sooner. Rewards \& benefits Explore the rewards and benefits that help you thrive – at every stage of your life and your career. Enjoy competitive salaries and a brilliant range of health, wellbeing, financial and lifestyle benefits. Make the most of a myriad of opportunities for training and professional development to grow your skills and expertise. And combine our hybrid working culture, international remote working opportunities, and flexible holiday allowances to balance a great job and fulfilling personal life. Be rewarded. Find out more. **About AtkinsRéalis** We're AtkinsRéalis, a world\-class engineering services and nuclear organization. We connect people, data and technology to transform the world's'infrastructure and energy systems. Together, with our industry partners and clients, and our global team of consultants, designers, engineers and project managers, we can change the world. We're committed to leading our clients across our various end markets to engineer a better future for our planet and its people. Find out more. Additional information We are committed to creating a culture where everyone feels that they belong \- a place where we can all be ourselves, thrive and develop to be the best we can be. So, we offer a range of family friendly, inclusive employment policies, flexible working arrangements and employee resource groups to support all employees. As an Equal Opportunities Employer, we value applications from all backgrounds, cultures and ability. **Salary** : €38,000 **Location** : AtkinsRéalis House, 150 Airside Business Park, Swords, Co Dublin, K67K5W4 **Hours worked:** 37\.5 hours per week

title: Graduate Site Engineer
description: **Graduate / Junior Site Engineer – Civil Infrastructure (Dublin)** A well\-established, mid\-sized civil engineering contractor is seeking a Graduate / Junior Site Engineer to join their growing team across Dublin and surrounding areas. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or junior engineer to gain hands\-on experience delivering a wide range of civil infrastructure projects, including roadworks, drainage, utilities, and public realm improvements. Working alongside experienced engineers and site management, the successful candidate will play a key role in supporting the delivery of high\-quality projects in a collaborative and fast\-paced environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with day\-to\-day site operations on civil infrastructure projects, including roads, drainage, utilities, and public realm works * Support setting out activities using GPS and total station equipment under supervision * Monitor and record daily site progress, maintaining accurate site diaries and documentation * Liaise with subcontractors, suppliers, and site teams to ensure smooth project delivery * Ensure works are carried out in accordance with drawings, specifications, and quality standards * Support the implementation of health \& safety procedures and site compliance requirements * Assist senior engineers and project managers in delivering projects on time and within budget * Contribute to quality assurance checks and preparation of as\-built records **Requirements** * Degree in Civil Engineering or a related discipline (recent graduates or final\-year students considered) * Previous site experience through internship, placement, or summer work in construction/civil engineering (Irish or UK experience preferred) * Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn on\-site engineering practices * Good communication and teamwork skills * Competent in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) * Basic understanding of surveying and setting out is desirable * Full driving licence (preferred but not essential) **What’s on Offer** * Competitive salary (€40,000 – €45,000 DOE) * Structured training and mentoring from experienced engineers * Exposure to a diverse range of civil infrastructure projects across Dublin * Clear career progression within a stable and growing contractor * Supportive team environment with strong emphasis on professional development **To Apply** If you are a recent Civil Engineering graduate or a Junior Engineer with site experience seeking a new opportunity in Dublin, please contact Amy Maguire for a confidential discussion or send your CV. Email: a.maguire@gpcireland.com GPC will never send your CV to clients without your permission. GPC is a specialist construction and engineering recruitment consultancy based in Dublin with over 27 years’ experience placing professionals across Ireland and internationally. title: HV Transmission Engineer
description: At Mace, our purpose is to redefine the boundaries of ambition. We believe in creating places that are responsible, bringing transformative impact to our people, communities, and societies across the globe. To learn more about our purpose, culture, and priorities, visit our strategy site. Within our consult business, we harness our unique combination of leading\-edge practical expertise and project delivery consultancy to unlock the potential in every project. The project: The role is within the technical design authority for the transmission system. Engineering and asset management is the area that facilitates that role through the development of technical standards and functional specifications for all plants to be connected to the transmission system and the development of new and innovative technologies and methodologies. In addition, the area is responsible for ensuring that requirements are incorporated in project design and construction whether the works are carried out by ESB networks or by third parties with medium voltage and high voltage projects. Our values shape the way we consult, and define the people we want to join us on our journey:* Safety first + Going home safe and well * Client focus \- Deliver on our promise * Integrity \- Always do the right thing * Create opportunity + for our people to excel You will be responsible for: * Ensuring that technical queries raised during the project are dealt with in accordance with TSO standards and policies and are closed out efficiently. * Ensuring that projects are carried out in a manner that meets requirements. * Reviewing and comparing submitted detailed designs against functional requirements and outline designs. * Monitoring of construction progress, and onsite inspection to ensure compliance with standards, functional requirements, and outline designs. * Ensuring developments are commissioned to comply with the requirements by producing and issuing commissioning instructions * Verifying transmission system requirements are met to allow commissioning tests to proceed. * Assisting the lead engineers with the engagement with customer and stakeholder technical consultants as and when required in order to resolve issues arising. * Coordinating with peers to meet business priorities, manage issues as they arise, provide technical guidance, and act as an escalation point to EAM manager. * Managing interactions on own projects with client engineering vendors, customers, and internal stakeholders. * Providing technical direction and assistance across the organisation on own projects and to team members and junior staff, and act as a team representative when required. * Ensuring the completion of stage gate reviews on all customer projects to ensure that the requirements of functional specifications are met. * Providing completion and dissemination of lessons learned on each project post project energisation. * Ensuring appropriate engagement with our partners in ESB networks including meetings, reporting, relationship management, and general communication as applicable to the client engineering role. * Facilitating and inputting project reviews, assessment, and auditing by internal and external parties as required. You’ll need to have: * You have an electrical engineering degree or equivalent with suitable experience within MV/HV projects. * You have transmission substation design expertise with experience assessing substation designs for conformity to standards at 110/220/400 kV (MV, HV). * You are digitally savvy, can build lasting client relationships, and lead strong, motivated teams. * You will have a construction or project management background and be hungry to grow your career and enjoy the opportunities Mace has to offer. You will have experience completing onsite inspections and monitoring construction progress for compliance to ensure transmission assets can be commissioned in accordance with standards. * Experience preparing and issuing transmission design drawings, such as single line diagrams, substation layout drawings, protection schematics and interlocking drawings. You’ll also have: * Membership of RICS, CIOB, APM, ICE or equivalent demonstrable professional or personal development. * Substantial experience in the successful delivery phase of projects and programmes in the energy sector. * Property or infrastructure project management expertise. * Commercial and financial acumen. * Experience of leading parts of projects within large, diverse teams. * Experience of developing and forming relationships with senior stakeholders.Knowledge of how carbon and sustainability considerations will shape the outlook of the projects you are working on. Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all of the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organization. \#LI\-Hybrid

title: ICT Cloud & Security Engineer
description: PFH Technology are currently looking for an **ICT Cloud \& Security Engineer** to join our growing team. **Location:** Dublin 1, hybrid **Contract length** : 12\-months (with expected extensions) **Responsibilities:** The ideal candidate will have at least 10\-years’ experience in the following: * Configure and manage Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) for identity governance, conditional access, and MFA. * Implement Privileged Identity Management (PIM) and role\-based access controls. * Deploy and manage Microsoft Intune for device compliance, application deployment, and endpoint configuration. * Configure \& manage Microsoft Defender XDR solutions (Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Office 365 etc). * Monitor and respond to security alerts using Microsoft Sentinel and other SIEM tools. * Apply best practices for securing Azure resources, including network security groups, firewalls, and encryption. * Implement Zero Trust principles across identity, devices, and applications. * Lead investigations for security incidents and provide remediation strategies. * Maintain detailed documentation for configurations, policies, and procedures. * Must be able to show a high degree of awareness of cybersecurity best practices and concepts including phishing awareness, Identity and Access Management, Multifactor Authentication, Zero Trust Security Model, Purview specially Information Protection * Must have demonstrated experience of threat evaluation and security incident response * Must have exposure to and be able to demonstrate experience using an ITIL aligned Service desk and associated principles such as Incident and Service request management, Problem Management, Change Management, continual Service improvement etc. * Must be able to demonstrate excellent support \& troubleshooting skills for Level 3 Incident Management aligned with ITIL best practices (Incident management, Service Request and Problem Management). **Duties:** * Be willing and capable to work well within a team environment * Be able to clearly communicate technical solutions in a user friendly and professional manner demonstrating a customer service mind\-set * Provide third line support for ICT Operations and other ICT Teams * Liaise with other ICT technical team specialists within the tenderer’s organisation to complete projects, (the response should clearly detail the areas where the tenderer has technical expertise). * Be able to follow Incident Management Process. * Create and update our Knowledge Management system, SOP’s, and Runbooks. * To contribute effectively to organisational and team processes and adhere to policies including but not limited to Data Protection and Information Security * Be responsible for ensuring the IT systems and applications are maintained to best practice. * Recognise system deficiencies and suggest effective solutions while complying with company policies and procedures. * Research current best practice technology solutions and translate them into our environment * To participate in Change Advisory Board (CAB) and Problem Management meetings and to share information with the support teams. * Be responsible for resolution of all tickets within agreed KPI timelines. * Carry out any other duties as required. **Education:** Must hold \- Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate AZ\-500 Must hold at least one of the following but all three would be advantageous: * Microsoft Certified: Security Operations Analyst Associate SC\-200 * Microsoft Certified: Identity and Access Administrator Associate SC\-300 * Microsoft Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate MD\-102 PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at PFH. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, disability, civil or family status, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, religion, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to ensuring that individuals with disabilities or access needs are provided with reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, to perform essential job functions, and to access the full benefits of employment. Please contact us if you require accommodation at any stage.

title: ICU/Anaesthetic Registrar
description: ### **Job Description** **Job Title:** **ICU/Anaesthetic Registrar** **Department:** **Anaesthesia and Intensive Care** **Reports to: Clinically and professionally responsible to the Director of Intensive Care or Daily Duty Intensivist. /Administratively accountable to the Head of the NCHD Office** **Date: 2026** **Overall Purpose of Job** -------------------------- The mission of Beacon Hospital is to provide quality care to all patients, in a respectful, safe and compassionate environment. In exercising this mission, Beacon Hospital will adhere to all health and safety regulations. To provide care to patients in the Operating Theatre and Critical Care Units, and to any other patients in the Hospital who require acute medical intervention that can be provided by an Anaesthetic Registrar, which includes, but is not limited to: Emergency situations including airway management. Daily review of all the patients in the Intensive Care Unit. Participation in the Intensive Care Unit Ward Rounds. Transfer if necessary patients for ICU admission from theatre. Follow up of post\-operative surgical patients. Acute decompensation of Medical patients. Any patients with fluid management issues including difficult vascular access. Cardiopulmonary resuscitation. Acute pain management. For assistance in theatre as required under consultant supervision. The Anaesthesia and Intensive Care Medicine Department at Beacon Hospital is a consultant delivered service. The team consists of the Anaesthetic Consultant who works in collaboration with Anaesthetic Registrars in patient care and in formulating management plans for patients. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables** ----------------------------------------- To act under the supervision of the Daily Duty Intensivist/Anaesthetist in the provision of an Intensive Care service and to support the Daily Duty Intensivist in their duties. At 07:00 and /or 19:00 go to the Critical Care Unit and liaise with the Nurse Manager or Nurse in Charge. Take hand over of patients from previous shift Registrar, so that continuity of care is ensured and immediate decisions are made and orders written. To review patients in the Critical Care Unit on a regular basis in accordance with the plan of care made at the beginning of the shift and inform the consultant of any issues or problems. Document review findings in clinical notes in a timely and legible manner. To attend to emergencies when they occur or free up the consultant to attend to emergencies. To respond to any adverse occurrences e.g. injuries, falls, sudden illnesses, etc suffered by patients, visitors or staff. To contribute to quality improvement; risk management initiatives and policy development so that the overall Anaesthesia Service is of the highest quality and reflective of best practice. To assist in the education of Critical Care colleagues and staff. To participate in work related projects at Beacon Hospital. To act and behave professionally and perform one's duties in a manner that optimises patient care and protects and enhances the reputation of Beacon Hospital. To adhere to Hospital Policies and Procedures e.g. JCI, Code Blue, infection control, work hours and overtime. To comply with Health and Safety Regulations, ensuring safe working practices in a safe working environment. To undertake any other duties that may be required, which could include assisting medical colleagues throughout the hospital and acting as a resource for more junior colleagues. **Person Specification** **Qualifications:** * MCAI, FCAI, MRCP or MRCS equivalent is required. * Current Full Irish Medical Council Registration. * Membership of the College of Anaesthetists is desirable. **Experience:** * At least 2 years' experience as an Anaesthetic Registrar in an acute hospital setting. **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge:** * Current ACLS certification required. * Current PALS certification required. **Personal Competencies:** * Proven ability to work as an effective team member. * Ability to communicate effectively. * All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or Hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: IPG Reporting Analyst
description: Investigator Payments Reporting Analyst \- Dublin \- Office Hybrid ICON plc is a world\-leading healthcare intelligence and clinical research organization. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment driving innovation and excellence, and we welcome you to join us on our mission to shape the future of clinical development. **The Role** The Investigator Payments Group (IPG) is the investigator payments function for ICON Clinical Research. The department role is to ensure timely, accurate and efficient execution of investigator payments \& to ensure all payments are carried out in accordance with ICON policies and procedures. **The Analyst Role Is To** * Support reporting requirements ensuring compliance with any Healthcare reporting legislation * Support operational reporting for IPG team **Role Responsibilities Include** * Recognise, exemplify and adhere to ICON's values which centre around our commitment to People, Clients and Performance. * Complete reports and tracking in a timely and consistent manner with concise, accurate information in various systems and tracking tools. * Ensure proper documentation and record tracking of meetings, decisions, interventions and escalations. * Liaise with customers to determine report requirements. * Interrogate \& analyse data and prepare relevant metrics. Accurate analysis with limited supervision is expected. * Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines, where necessary * Become proficient in systems, tools and ICON’s applications to efficiently and accurately analyse data. * Perform other reasonably related duties, analysis and special projects as assigned by management team. * As a member of staff, the employee is expected to embrace and contribute to our culture of process improvement with a focus on streamlining our processes adding value to our business and meeting client needs. **To be successful in the role, you will ideally have:** * Bachelor’s degree in finance, data analytics, business administration, or a related field * Prior relevant experience related to data analysis and production of reports is essential * Advanced excel skills are essential (Pivot tables, lookups, etc.) * Demonstrate a logical approach to analysis with a questioning attitude * Numerate with some financial understanding * Excellent working knowledge of MS office package * Excellent communication (written and oral), organisational and customer service skills are essential * Self\-starter who can work with minimal supervision **What ICON Can Offer You** Our success depends on the quality of our people. That’s why we’ve made it a priority to build a diverse culture that rewards high performance and nurtures talent. In addition to your competitive salary, ICON offers a range of additional benefits. Our benefits are designed to be competitive within each country and are focused on well\-being and work life balance opportunities for you and your family. **Our Benefits Examples Include** * Various annual leave entitlements * A range of health insurance offerings to suit you and your family’s needs. * Competitive retirement planning offerings to maximize savings and plan with confidence for the years ahead. * Global Employee Assistance Programme, LifeWorks, offering 24\-hour access to a global network of over 80,000 independent specialized professionals who are there to support you and your family’s well\-being. * Life assurance * Flexible country\-specific optional benefits, including childcare vouchers, bike purchase schemes, discounted gym memberships, subsidized travel passes, health assessments, among others. Visit our careers site to read more about the benefits ICON offers. At ICON, inclusion \& belonging are fundamental to our culture and values. We’re dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible environment for all candidates. ICON is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination and harassment. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please let us know or submit a request here Interested in the role, but unsure if you meet all of the requirements? We would encourage you to apply regardless – there’s every chance you’re exactly what we’re looking for here at ICON whether it is for this or other roles. Are you a current ICON Employee? Please click here to apply

title: IRE_QA Warehouse Specialist
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Bridge to thousands of biopharma companies and their patients. **We are PCI.** Our investment is in People who make an impact, drive progress and create a better tomorrow. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. We are looking for a QA Warehouse Specialist to join our team in Dundalk. * Sample, approve, and release incoming goods, packaging materials, and products (where required) for use in production * Request and review Certificates of Analysis (CoA) / Certificates of Conformance (CoC) for incoming goods, packaging materials, and products * Review and file acetates for use during sampling activities * Identify training needs and conduct staff training as required * Maintain sampling documentation and archive retain samples * Manage the Supplier/Customer Defect (SCD) system, including issuance of SCD numbers, coordination with customers and suppliers, and closure of SCDs * Liaise with customers and suppliers on artwork\-related queries and manage artwork approvals * Review and approve Master Packaging Material Specifications * Ensure high standards of housekeeping; conduct housekeeping audits and prepare reports * Raise, investigate, and close deviations and Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA) as required * Ensure all activities are conducted in compliance with current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) requirements * Assist in validation studies as required * Ensure safe work practices are followed at all times, report safety issues immediately to the Safety Officer, and attend Health and Safety meetings * Perform other duties as required from time to time in line with the overall goals of the company * Essential: + Relevant experience may be considered in lieu of formal educational requirements + Proven track record of delivering results + Ability to work toward clearly defined goals, communicate progress effectively, and meet predefined targets + Bachelor’s or Associate Degree, or Diploma/Certificate in Quality, Pharmaceutical Science, or a related discipline + One to two years of experience working in Quality / QA + Ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritize effectively based on business needs + Strong problem\-solving and troubleshooting skills, with a dynamic and proactive approach + Self\-motivated with a results\-driven mindset + Flexibility to work across different shifts as required in line with business needs + Adaptable and able to work collaboratively within cross\-functional teams + Proficient in Microsoft Office applications (including Excel, PowerPoint, Word, etc.) + Willingness to travel between sites based on company requirements Join us and be part of building the bridge between life changing therapies and patients. Let’s talk future **Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Statement** *PCI Pharma Services is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We do not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, age, sex, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, marital or domestic or civil union status, familial status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetics, disability, military eligibility or veteran status, or any other protected status.* *At PCI, Equity and Inclusion are at the core of our company’s purpose: Together, delivering life\-changing therapies. We are committed to cultivating an inclusive workplace by holding ourselves accountable to the highest standards of understanding, fairness, respect, and equal opportunity – at every level. We envision a PCI community where everyone can belong and grow, and we strive to bring this vision to reality by continuously and intentionally assessing our people practices, policies and programs, marketing approach, and workplace culture.*

title: Independent Consultant in Finance
description: At Hetmos Group, we do not recruit on resumes. We partner on networks. If you move in circles where trust, discretion, and quality matter, we offer a long\-term collaboration model built on luxury standards, across three high\-value verticals. ***About the Hetmos Group*** • **Hetmos M\&A** : mergers and acquisitions for small and lower mid\-cap companies (tech, industrial, services) • **Hetestate** : strategic and off\-market real estate transactions • **Hetwealth** : wealth structuring for executives, entrepreneurs, and investors Our DNA: excellence, discretion, selectivity, and high\-quality relationships. ***Your role ﻿*** As an Independent Mandate Partner, you are a trusted entry point to high\-value opportunities. You introduce the group to CEOs, shareholders, investors, high\-net\-worth individuals, and strategic partners in your ecosystem, and initiate qualified conversations. You open the doors. Our internal teams handle execution. ***Who we work with*** • **Experienced profiles** : consultants, bankers, brokers, financial advisors, lawyers, accountants, family office professionals, senior executives. • **Junior profiles** welcome if introduced or endorsed, with strong interpersonal skills and the ability to open qualified doors. No prior expertise in M\&A, real estate, or wealth is required. Full training and support provided. ***Collaboration model*** • Independent contractor • 100% success\-based, high commissions • No earnings cap • Paid only upon successful completion • Freedom to operate across one or several verticals ***Growth path*** Top performers access larger mandates, structure their own territory, and may be invited to equity participation. ***Interested?*** Interested in working with a demanding, discreet, and ambitious group? Send your profile, we review every application personally.

title: Intern, Software Engineer
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD92001 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. As a global leader in 3D design, engineering, and entertainment software, we help people everywhere imagine, design, and create a better world. Right now, we’re transforming how our products work by moving to the cloud—opening up exciting opportunities for collaboration, innovation, and impact. What sets us apart isn’t just our technology, but our culture. At Autodesk, you’ll find an inclusive, supportive environment where early\-career talent can grow, be creative, and make real impact. It’s one of the reasons we’ve been consistently recognized by **Fortune, Forbes, and Glassdoor as a top place to work** . Over three months in our Summer 2026 Internship Programme, you’ll gain hands\-on experience, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your engineering and domain expertise, while making real contributions to Autodesk’s manufacturing and construction technologies. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns, locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally, through programs designed just for interns. As a Software Engineer Intern on the Developer Enablement team, you’ll contribute directly to building and scaling applications that utilize environmental data, delivered through a unified standardized interface. You’ll receive mentorship, guidance, and support, while also having the autonomy and responsibility to make a real impact. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand the full software development lifecycle * Contribute to real commercial development projects, guided by experienced Autodesk developers * Gain hands\-on experience with TypeScript, AWS, automated testing frameworks, DevOps, and more * Apply Agile and Scrum practices, building your project management skills * Use source control and Continuous Integration platforms such as GitHub and Jenkins * Develop robust APIs to enable sustainability analysis for Autodesk customers * Share your progress and achievements in team meetings, gaining feedback and recognition **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a degree in a STEM field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) * Expected graduation date: please include your month and year of graduation in your application * Experience with API development (REST and GraphQL), through coursework, personal projects, or previous internships * Programming experience (TypeScript preferred) * Enthusiasm for learning new technologies and a proactive approach to problem\-solving **The Ideal Candidate** * Familiarity with automated testing principles to help write reliable, maintainable code * Familiarity with AWS or other cloud platforms, giving you a head start in cloud\-based development * Additional experience with other programming languages or frameworks is a plus * Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team environment * Previous internship experience in a related field is advantageous Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

title: International Freight Forwarding Logistics Specialist – Expo & Event
description: **Company Description** Interflow Logistics is a specialist international freight forwarder focused on the delivery of **high‑stakes, time‑sensitive shipments for live events worldwide** . We work behind the scenes on some of the world’s most demanding projects, where accuracy, timing and coordination are critical to success. Our core areas of expertise include: * **Trade Shows \& Exhibitions** – Managing international transport, customs clearance and on‑site logistics for global events * **Live Events** – Delivering logistics solutions for large‑scale corporate and public events * **Sports Logistics** – Handling specialist transport and on‑site support for professional sports bodies around the world From navigating complex customs regulations to providing hands‑on support on the ground, Interflow is trusted to deliver where failure is not an option. **Role Description** We’re looking to bring someone new into Interflow who is **early in their career, eager to learn, and genuinely interested in international events** . The **International Freight Forwarding Logistics Specialist – Expo \& Event** will support the planning and coordination of logistics services for exhibitions and live events. This is a **hands‑on, fast‑paced role** , working closely with experienced colleagues, international partners and clients. This is **not a standard 9–5 logistics position** . Live events are dynamic by nature, and flexibility, curiosity and calm problem‑solving are essential. This is a full‑time, on‑site role based in **Dublin** . **Candidates must be currently living in Ireland and available to start immediately. Visa sponsorship is not available for this role.** **Key Responsibilities** * Coordinating international freight forwarding for exhibitions and live events * Supporting export and import processes, customs clearance and compliance * Liaising with clients, overseas agents, airlines and transport providers * Tracking shipments and resolving issues in real time * Supporting on‑site logistics during event delivery where required * Providing a high level of service in a deadline‑driven environment **Who This Role Is Suited To** This role is ideal for: * A **recent graduate** or someone at an **early stage of their logistics career** * Someone interested in **events, exhibitions or international projects** * A person who enjoys problem‑solving and variety rather than routine work **Salary \& Benefits** * **Competitive entry‑level salary** in line with experience and potential * Typical starting range: **€40,000 – €45,000 gross per annum** * Clear opportunity for **salary progression** as experience and responsibility increase * 22 days annual leave plus public holidays * Pension scheme after qualifying period * Exposure to international events and travel opportunities over time This role is designed as a development position, with increased responsibility and remuneration as experience is gained. Why Join Interflow At Interflow, you won’t be managing routine shipments you’ll be contributing to the successful delivery of international exhibitions, live events and sports projects where timing is critical and no two days are the same. This role offers hands‑on learning, close collaboration with experienced colleagues, and the opportunity to build a long‑term career in specialist event logistics within a fast‑paced, professional environment.

title: Investment Analyst - Investment Analytics
description: We are currently looking for an experienced Investment Analyst to join the Investment Analytics and Reporting team at Mediolanum. The Investment Analytics and Reporting team are responsible for preparing critiques and insights into target portfolios, having a clear position analysis on all target funds. In this role, the successful candidate will work directly with Portfolio Managers to scenario test and to assess risk across portfolios ensuring there are tolerances. You will also support in the process of ensuring the right Performance Measurement \& Portfolio analysis tools, people and processes are in place to support portfolio critiques. This is an exceptional opportunity to become part of a vibrant, high\-performing team at a leading asset manager based in Dublin. **Principal Duties of the position, including but not limited to:** * Oversee the Performance attribution and Portfolio analysis management software tools with a view to improve the sophistication of the data integration. * Provide critiques and insight about the performance of target portfolios from analysing numbers, market knowledge and using tools to complete risk management and scenario testing. * Present critique and insight at committee level regarding Portfolio analysis. * Use visually representative tools to create data that is consumable by portfolio managers. * Work closely with Portfolio Managers to improve understanding of portfolios and risk scenarios. * Build out and oversees the Performance Measurement and Portfolio Analysis team. **Skills and Competencies:** * Ability to interact with all stakeholders at various levels to ensure efficient delivery of business requests. * A working knowledge of SQL and Business Intelligence tools such as Microsoft BI. **Qualifications and Experience:** * A Bachelor’s Honours or Masters Degree in Finance, Business, Quantitative Finance, Engineering or Risk Management. * At least 5 years experience providing portfolio analysis and related reporting directly to front office investment personnel, preferably at an asset manager. (Flexibility with experience for the right candidate). * Experience with one or more of Bloomberg PORT, Style Research, Morningstar, MSCI risk analytics, or MYSIS Fusion Invest is a distinct advantage. * Knowledge of financial markets, exposure to different asset classes and securities via different instruments. * Knowledge or experience with scripted languages (e.g. R, Matlab, Python) and data science exposure is also required. * Understanding of requirements under different regulatory regimes, including Solvency II, UCITS \& MIFID is advantageous. * Pursuing certifications such as CFA, CAIA, FRM or CIPM is a distinct advantage.

title: Investment Associate
description: **About us** At Davy, it’s the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team – you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. An opportunity has arisen for an **Investment Associate** within the Private Clients department at Davy. **About the role** * Working closely with an Associate Director/Director: * Assist in the creation and execution of relationship plans for each client relating to the implementation of their financial plan, adherence with service standards which lead to the development of long\-term client relationships; * Identifying revenue potential and opportunities to cross\-sell Davy services; * Review existing client relationships, and in line with Investment Strategy, analyse whether the relationships are categorised correctly and therefore managed appropriately; * Execute a engagement plan for each advisory relationship to ensure they receive proactive, relevant and appropriate contact, investment ideas and recommendations and structured portfolio reviews; * Managing MI relating to client relationships; * Deal promptly and efficiently with client queries; * Ensure that all activities and duties are carried out in full compliance with regulatory requirements, Davy’s Risk Management Framework and internal Policies and Policy Standards; * Be recognised as an expert and influential contact for Davy’s services and in relation to client queries; * Ability to network with peers at industry events. * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role **Requirements** **About you** * Degree or Masters in Business related area; * 2\-3 years industry experience; * QFA qualification; * A high degree of proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word and PowerPoint; * Proficient in CRM; * A commitment to further education and continuous learning; * Demonstrable interest and understanding of investments; * A strong client focus, to ensure effective service delivery; * Strong communication and presentation skills; * Excellent attention to detail; * Excellent organisational skills; * An ability to prioritise own workload. **What we offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today’s fast\-moving world, we know it’s important to feel secure and empowered. That’s why Davy benefits support you through all of life’s stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. * **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. * **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. * **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. * **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. * **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values – One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don’t meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group. *The position set out above is defined as a “Controlled Function (CF)” role, namely CF3, CF4, CF10\.under Section 20 of the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 and Schedule 1 of the Regulations (S.I. No. 437 of 2011\) as amended. In accordance with Section 21 of the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 and the Fitness and Probity Standards 2014, any offer of employment is conditional upon you demonstrating that you meet the Fitness \& Probity requirements, applicable to this position. The role is also subject to the Minimum Competency Code and therefore there is a requirement to hold, or be willing to take the APA (Regulation \& Investment) or (Pensions) or Registered Stockbroker or QFA exams.*
