title: Junior Security Operations Engineer
description: **About Telnyx** *Telnyx is an industry leader that's not just imagining the future of global connectivity—we're building it. From architecting and amplifying the reach of a* *private, global, multi\-cloud IP network* *, to bringing* *hyperlocal edge* *technology right to your fingertips through intuitive APIs, we're shaping a new era of seamless interconnection between people, devices, and applications.* *We're driven by a desire to transform and modernize what's antiquated, automate the manual, and solve real\-world problems through innovative connectivity solutions. As a testament to our success, we're proud to stand as a financially stable and profitable company. Our robust profitability allows us not only to invest in pioneering technologies but also to foster an environment of continuous learning and growth for our team.* *Our collective vision is a world where borderless connectivity fuels limitless innovation. By joining us, you can be part of laying the foundations for this interconnected future. We're currently seeking passionate individuals who are excited about the opportunity to contribute to an industry\-shaping company while growing their own skills and careers.* **About The Role** We’re looking for a Junior Security Operations Engineer who is AI\-Forward to help scale and modernize our SecOps program. This is a hands\-on, builder role for someone who will design and ship the security tooling that powers our triage, investigations, and response workflows. You’ll report to the Technical Operations Director and work alongside our GRC lead to improve our vulnerability intake, threat response, darkweb posture, and internal security tooling. A core part of this role is building AI\-assisted security tooling: triage agents that pre\-classify bug bounty reports, investigation copilots that pull context from logs and SIEM data, response workflows that draft remediation steps and track them to closure. You’ll spend as much time wiring up that tooling as you will reproducing vulnerabilities and working incidents. This role suits someone who thrives in a lean, high\-impact environment, has strong opinions on where humans add value versus where tooling should take over, and wants to shape how a modern security team operates. **What You’ll Do** **Triage \& Vulnerability Management** * Review incoming vulnerability reports from our bug bounty intake; reproduce and document valid issues for engineering teams. * Build the tooling that improves signal\-to\-noise: automate duplicate detection, spam filtering, and abuse flagging so humans only touch what’s worth touching. * Act on DAST findings: coordinate remediation, verify fixes, and re\-test. * Track remediation timelines for critical vulnerabilities and keep stakeholders honest on SLAs. **Threat Response \& Monitoring** * Monitor and respond to EDR and cloud security alerts; investigate, contain, and document. * Analyze darkweb findings and credential exposures; shape our darkweb monitoring practices and tooling over time. * Improve detection coverage: tune noisy rules, close gaps, and enrich alerts with context so responders spend time on decisions, not data gathering. * Help configure and tune DLP, SIEM, and AI security tooling. **Security Tooling (core to this role)** * Build AI\-assisted triage tooling that pre\-classifies bug bounty reports, flags duplicates, and routes real issues to the right engineering team with reproduction context attached. * Build investigation tooling: LLM\-backed copilots and Slack bots that pull alert context, correlate signals across sources, and surface what actually matters. * Build response tooling: workflows that draft remediation steps, generate evidence artifacts, and track issues to closure across the teams that own them. * Evaluate emerging AI security tooling and bring what’s genuinely useful into the stack; retire what isn’t. * Apply a security\-minded lens to our own AI usage: prompt injection, data exfiltration, model misuse, and the guardrails that go around them. **Compliance \& Cross\-Functional** * Support audit evidence collection for SOC 2, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS. * Partner with ITOps to verify patches, endpoint coverage, and access hygiene. **You May Be a Good Fit If You Have** * Previous experience in a SecOps, Security Analyst, or Threat Response role. * Proven ability to understand and reproduce technical vulnerabilities. You can read a bug report and tell whether it’s real. * Experience with bug bounty triage (HackerOne, Bugcrowd, or similar). * Hands\-on exposure to SIEM, EDR, and DLP tools in production environments. * A genuine, demonstrated interest in applying AI/LLMs to security work: side projects, internal tooling, prompt engineering, agent frameworks, anything that shows you’ve actually built with this stuff. * Scripting and automation skills in Python, Bash, or similar. You reach for code before you reach for a checklist. * Comfort working autonomously across time zones and asynchronously. * Strong written communication. You can turn a messy investigation into a clear, defensible writeup. **Bonus Points For** * Experience building LLM agents, RAG pipelines, or Slack bots backed by OpenAI / Anthropic / open\-source models. * Detection engineering experience (Sigma rules, custom Falcon queries, Grafana/Loki dashboards). * Cloud security depth in AWS or GCP (IAM, workload posture, cloud\-native logging). * Prior work in regulated environments (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS) from the practitioner side. * Exposure to AI/ML security concerns such as prompt injection, model evals, and data leakage through LLMs. * Public contributions: blog posts, CVEs, open\-source tools, CTF writeups. **Engagement \& Logistics** * Full\-time contract. * Remote\-first and async\-friendly. We have hubs in San Francisco, Denver, Dublin, and Amsterdam. If you’re near one, we’d love to see you in the office regularly; if you’re elsewhere in the world, that works too. * Potential to extend or convert based on fit. * Reports to the Technical Operations Director; works closely with the GRC lead and IT Operations. **How We Work** We’re a lean, high\-trust team. We value people who ship, who can operate independently, and who treat security as an engineering problem rather than a checklist. If you’re someone who sees a repetitive task and immediately thinks “this should be a script, or better yet, an agent,” you’ll fit in here. **To Apply** Tell us about a time you used AI, automation, or custom tooling to meaningfully change how a security workflow ran. What was manual before, what it looked like after, and what you learned. Links to code, writeups, or demos are welcome.

Training will be provided. * Experience with document management systems is an advantage (e.g., Zutec, DFM, Obi, or similar). * Must be proficient in Microsoft Office. * Must have an Irish/EU work permit. **Desirable:** * Previous experience in a manufacturing engineering environment is desirable. * A portfolio of work demonstrating 3D modelling proficiency is favourable. **Benefits:** * On\-site parking available * Early Friday finish 4:30pm * Career development opportunities available **Schedule:** * 08:00 – 17:00 Monday – Thursday * 08:00 – 16:30 Friday **Salary:** * €30,000 \- €35,000 based on experience and negotiable for the right candidate. **Location:** * Extreme Balconies is ideally located in Newcastle Dublin, 5km from the N7 Junction 4\. On\-site parking is available. title: Junior UX/UI designer
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Shape high\-leverage product experiences.** Design exceptional UI and UX—from early concepts to polished delivery—using the best available tools to explore more directions, move faster, and raise the quality bar. Ensure every experience is intuitive, effective, and delightful. * **Start with an LLM.** Use AI tools to clarify intent, draft short specs, and surface risks, edge cases, and initial approaches before opening any design tool. Use this work to align quickly with your team and compress the time from ambiguity to direction. * **Prototype and build with AI.** Use Cursor and Claude Code to turn rough concepts into interactive prototypes and generate production\-ready frontend code. You know when to reach for code and when to reach for Figma—and you move fluidly between both to get the best result. * **Co\-create with PMs and engineers from day one.** Define UX requirements early, stay in the build through QA and release, and make engineering\-aware design decisions. * **Validate and measure.** Talk directly with customers, run quick tests, and use what you learn to adjust direction. Track funnels, retention, and adoption to assess design effectiveness and sharpen what ships next. * **Find the highest\-leverage bets from the backlog.** Identify where product improvements can unlock business value, align stakeholders around them, and move fast to deliver. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Learning & Development Executive

We work collaboratively, bringing the best people and teams together to solve complex issues, seize opportunities, and create better outcomes for clients. The expectations of our clients, the competitive marketplace and our own ambitions have grown consistently over recent years. To ensure that we have the right structures in place to support the achievement of our goals we are currently recruiting for a **Learning \& Development Executive** . We wish to recruit an enthusiastic, motivated and adaptable person to join the Firm’s HR \& People Development team. The function of this role is to support the Legal and Professional Development team in delivering development programmes, enabling our internal talent to achieve their full potential. The role requires experience in the delivery of training and supporting performance management. Based in our Dublin office, the successful candidate will report to the Director of Legal and Professional Development and work closely with the wider HR and People Development team. Applications for appointment on a full\-time basis or a 4 day week basis will be considered. The Learning \& Development Executive will have responsibility for: * Manage and coordinate the delivery of MF Advance, our in\-house training programme delivered to trainee solicitors as part of the Law Society of Ireland’s training requirements. * Liaise, as required, with the Law Society, MF Advance subject leads and/or the external examiners on the MF Advance curriculum design, timetabling, trainee assessments and related issues. * Manage our established training programmes. * Create and develop further programmes for trainees and junior associates. * Support the trainee and associate performance review process. * Analyse individual personal development plans, focusing on the provision of meaningful solutions, including the design, delivery and evaluation of all training provided. * Provider management (sourcing, selecting, reviewing and negotiating costs). * Ensure that cost effective training is planned and delivered making the best use of internal/external resources available. * Provide support and feedback to employees as they engage in career planning and development work. * Support the annual early careers programmes. **Requirements:** The successful candidate will have: Education: * A third level qualification \- Training / Law/HR/Business/ Psychology or similar. Professional Experience: * The ideal candidate will have in the region of 3\-5 years' experience in a Learning \& Development role. * Experience working with management teams and building strong credible relationships. * Demonstrate the ability to work with and effectively communicate to all levels within an organisation and to deliver on learning targets in a cost effective and timely manner. * Previous experience within a professional services firm or similar would be advantageous. Knowledge \& Expertise: * Experience in design and delivery of workshops and other training formats; * Ability to engage with senior stakeholders to scope, design and implement group learning events; * Experience of exploring and assessing development needs with individuals; * Experience of carrying out observation and giving feedback; * Qualified to use psychometric tools would be advantageous. Skills: * Strong interpersonal, facilitation and mentoring skills are essential; * Demonstrate strong written and oral communication skills; * A high degree of discretion; * A positive attitude and a collaborative approach to working; * Digitally literate, with an interest in emerging technologies, including Generative AI. **Our Offer** Competitive salary Hybrid working options Pension contributions Life assurance and income protection cover Relocation assistance (for those joining from overseas) A great working environment A lively sports and social club **Applications** To apply for this role, please email careers@mccannfitzgerald.com with your CV and, for a confidential discussion or for more information, please contact Eimear Power (Recruitment Manager) on \+353 1 607 12 92

title: Legal PA, Litigation & Dispute Management - Dublin
description: We are Eversheds Sutherland, a global law firm, with more than 5,000 people across over 30 countries. We’re full\-service with deep niche and sector experience. Whatever challenge, wherever in the world, we’re equipped and ready to meet it. We live our values, we’re purposeful and purpose\-led. So although the world is fast\-moving and rapidly changing, we see it as a place where everyone can thrive. We’re ambitious for our clients, our communities – and for you. Whether you’re starting out on your career or well established, whether you’re a lawyer or in business services. If you’re looking for what’s next, we are too. **What To Expect** As a Legal PA within our Practice Group Support team, your role is pivotal. You’ll provide confidential, professional and proactive support to a designated group of stakeholders. Confidence, effective communication and influential skills are essential. We value an assertive and forward\-thinking approach. Here’s what to expect: **Diverse responsibilities:** Our Legal PAs handle a wide range of tasks, supporting various aspects of our business. Empowerment and mentorship: You’ll lead by example, fostering ongoing personal and professional development among junior colleagues and peers. **Continuous learning:** We invest in your professional development through our development plans. We offer personalised mentoring and coaching, technical training and soft skills courses to boost motivation and success in your career path. **Culture of feedback:** Our culture thrives on continuous feedback that builds confidence, development and recognition of achievements. **Pro Bono, Community and Diversity \& Inclusion (D\&I) opportunities:** Beyond your daily tasks, we encourage you to contribute to Pro Bono, Community and D\&I initiatives. Make a difference by using your skills to benefit our communities. **Ambition for people, clients and communities:** Our ambition extends beyond business success. We are equally committed to the growth and well\-being of our people, clients and the broader community. **Staying ahead:** Collaborate closely with lawyers, take ownership of tasks and exceed business expectations, anticipate challenges, adapt to changing technologies. in the legal sector. For talented, ambitious individuals there's no better place to be working in the law industry. **Role And Key Responsibilities** **Stakeholder engagement:** Conduct regular one to one meetings with stakeholders to discuss actions, priorities, expectations and future planning. Attend team meetings to stay informed and contribute effectively. Act as a liaison point for both internal and external clients, draft clear and concise replies to incoming correspondence. Assist with client queries during stakeholder absences. Maintain a flexible approach and adopt hybrid working to accommodate stakeholder and client needs. **Adaptability and collaboration:** Demonstrate client service excellence by being responsive to stakeholder requests. Support the wider PA team, covering for team members during absences or when required to ensure continued resource. **Efficient mailbox, diary and travel management:** Proactively manage mailboxes and diaries including travel coordination. Anticipate future needs and take initiative to resolve conflicts. **Meeting organisation and coordination:** Draft agendas and prepare for internal and external meetings. Ensure necessary equipment and refreshments for both physical and virtual meetings using available technology and resources. **Workflow management and delegation:** Own the end\-to\-end process, be accountable for successful conclusion of all stakeholder requests. Delegate tasks effectively across dedicated support teams such as billing, finance, team assistants, document production to meet expectations and deadlines in line with the Scope of Service. **Document management:** Undertake urgent and short document work. Collaborate with the Document Production Team for larger documents utilising our dedicated software. **Matter management:** Manage the entire client and matter opening process including electronic filing, collaborating with stakeholders and other business support teams. **Financial administration and compliance:** Provide financial administrative support to stakeholders, coordinating with billing and finance teams, ensuring compliance with legal, firm\-wide and practice\-specific policies and procedures. **Pitch and presentation creation:** Collaborate with the document production team to create impactful pitches and PowerPoint presentations for stakeholders. **Marketing and business development:** Coordinate and organise events, create event material, digital working lists and communicate with attendees to ensure a seamless end to end process. **Championing process improvements:** Be an advocate for process changes within the organisation, influence decisions and be a part of the solution. **Efficient administration:** Manage electronic filing, records maintenance and expenses effectively using the firm's designated software. **Technology utilisation:** Leverage the firm’s technology as a key business tool for managing, updating, reporting and research. Create and maintain data sites for efficient collaboration. Seek opportunities to embrace technologies that improve our ways of working and drive productivity in day\-to\-day activities. **Skills And Experience** We are looking for: * Experience in a similar environment: Experience working within a comparable professional setting, providing seamless adaptation to our busy environment. * Client service excellence: Strive to exceed client expectations by delivering outstanding service. Anticipate client needs and proactively address any issues. * Problem\-solving and initiative: Be a proactive problem solver. Anticipate challenges, take initiative and implement solutions efficiently. Aspire for excellence in all tasks. Show dedication and commitment to achieving strategic goals. * Effective communication: Demonstrate excellent communication skills when liaising with stakeholders and clients. Your ability to convey information clearly and professionally is essential. * Delegation and ownership: Delegate tasks across dedicated support teams while maintaining overall ownership of transactional activities. * High\-level support: Provide exceptional PA support to senior stakeholders, including managing calendars, organising meetings and handling confidential information. * Relationship building: Cultivate strong relationships both internally and externally. Collaborate effectively with colleagues and clients. * Task management and risk awareness: Skilfully manage tasks with expertise, seeking ways to enhance efficiency and quality. Balance priorities and stay alert to risks, ensuring tasks are completed on time and stakeholder expectations are met. * Flexibility: Demonstrate flexibility and embrace change. Adapt seamlessly to evolving work environments. * Microsoft applications proficiency: Demonstrate intermediate to advanced skills in Microsoft applications including Outlook, Teams and Copilot. * Team collaboration: Contribute to a positive team environment. Offer support to colleagues when needed. **Diversity \& Inclusion** At Eversheds Sutherland, “Inclusive” is a core business value. We bring together different skillsets, global mindsets and approaches. We foster diversity of thought and the freedom to put ideas into action. We have an inherent respect for the individual. We have a strong belief in collaboration and teamwork. Sharing ideas, asking questions, solving challenges and meeting our clients’ goals: together. We want all our people to thrive at work and reach their full potential and we work hard to continue to build a diverse and inclusive culture, monitor and report on our progress and impact, and develop our approach. This is reflected in our policies, systems and processes, and in our work with diversity membership organisations. Many of our people work flexibly in some way and we are open to considering how we can accommodate flexible working arrangements alongside role requirements. If this is important to you, please talk to us about it during the recruitment process. We want you to perform at your best during our recruitment process. If there is any adjustment or support you need, please contact us so we can discuss how we can best assist you. **For you,** **For your success.** **And what's next.**

title: Looking to Progress Your Career? Unique Opportunity
description: **Looking to Progress Your Career? Unique Opportunity** ***Associate Recruitment Consultant – Dublin – Open to Career Changers*** * **Unique Leadership Academy – Our Leaders Grow Internally** * **Take your Career to the Next Level – Financial \& Career Progression** * **Carve your path and boost your career in a meritocratic organisation.** Looking to advance your career, learn new skills, and make a positive impact? When you join us as an Associate Recruitment Consultant in Dublin you have the opportunity to use your existing skills and be supported to develop a rewarding career with outstanding growing potential. You will connect great talent with meaningful employment and offer top\-quality consultancy to our clients. We believe in continuous professional development for all of our team members and therefore offer access to a training program to become a Certified Recruitment Consultant. Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, which come attached with uncapped earning potential. **Key Responsibilities:** * Collaborating with clients to understand their hiring needs and develop tailored recruitment strategies. * Sourcing, screening, and presenting qualified candidates to match client requirements. * Build and maintain lasting relationships with candidates, guiding them through the recruitment process. * Managing candidate portfolios, ensuring database management, compliance, and reference checks. **What We Look for in Our Teammates:** * Strong educational background. * 1\.5 to 3 years of professional experience in any field (such as Events, Sales, Customer Service, Banking, Retail, Insurance, etc…). * Proven ability to meet targets/ deadlines. * Thrives in a proactive and fast\-paced environment. **Salary \& Benefits We Offer:** * Continuous learning \& professional development \- renowned training programme plus Leadership Academy * Clear progression structure with set goals to achieve for each promotion – a true meritocracy * Flexible benefits package built by you * Dublin city location * Quarterly team events \+ wellness activities \+ Friday drinks For more information contact Francisca confidentiallly at \+353 1 960 9165 or email your CV to francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie **Please note that only EU Citizens \& Stamp 4 applications will be considered for this role.**

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Maintenance Support Engineer
description: **Maintenance Support Engineer – Syringe Filling Operations** **Position Summary** The Maintenance Department Support Engineer is responsible for day\-to\-day commercial support within the maintenance department. The role will span numerous areas such as, but not limited too, shutdown coordination, engineering reliability projects, supporting and leading all quality non\-conformances and leading maintenance equipment RCA’s, leading and coordinating all training\-related activities within the maintenance department of a pharmaceutical syringe filling plant. There will also be a training aspect of this role which ensures that maintenance personnel are effectively onboarded, trained, and continuously developed to support safe, compliant, and efficient manufacturing operations. The position also plays a key role in building and sustaining a high\-performing training hub aligned with site training academy initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Support Maintenance Reliability and Shutdown Planning * The Maintenance support engineer is responsible for improving the reliability, availability, and maintainability of plant equipment and utilities within the ADL manufacturing facility. The role focuses on leading and developing shutdown planning and sustaining a robust reliability framework that ensures right\-first\-time maintenance execution, minimises unplanned downtime, and optimises asset lifecycle performance in support of safe, compliant, and efficient operations. * The role partners closely with Maintenance, Engineering, Operations, and Quality to analyse equipment performance, identify failure modes, and implement preventive and predictive maintenance strategies. The Maintenance Reliability Engineer also leads continuous improvement initiatives to reduce non\-commercial time, improve equipment uptime, and increase overall plant availability. * Hiring \& Onboarding Coordination (Maintenance Department) * Coordinate all hiring logistics for maintenance technicians in partnership with HR and maintenance leadership. * Lead onboarding processes, ensuring a structured and compliant induction for all new hires. * Serve as the primary point of contact for new maintenance employees during onboarding. * Ensure all onboarding documentation, training assignments, and compliance requirements are completed on time. * Training Program Development (Syringe Filling Lines) * Design, develop, and implement technical training programs for new and existing syringe filling lines. * Collaborate with SMEs (Subject Matter Experts), engineering, and operations to ensure training content reflects current equipment, processes, and regulatory requirements. * Standardize training materials, work instructions, and qualification processes across all syringe lines. * Incorporate safety, GMP, and operational excellence principles into all training curricula. * Maintenance Technician Training \& Onboarding Program * Develop, implement, and continuously improve a structured onboarding and qualification program for new maintenance technicians. * Define competency frameworks, training pathways, and certification requirements. * Monitor trainee progress and provide feedback to supervisors and stakeholders. * Identify skill gaps and implement targeted development plans for both new and existing technicians. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical or Chemical) * Manufacturing experience in biopharmaceutical Formulation/Fill/Finish or Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) facilities * Solid leadership, technical writing, and communication/presentation skills * Strong understanding of GMP, regulatory requirements, and training compliance standards. * Proven experience in training program development, technical training, or learning \& development. * Experience working with maintenance teams, equipment, and troubleshooting processes is highly desirable. **Skills \& Competencies:** * Excellent strategic planning and demonstrated project management skills * Strong organizational and coordination skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities. * Instructional design and training delivery expertise. * Analytical mindset with the ability to track and improve training effectiveness. * Leadership capability with a focus on coaching and developing others. * High attention to detail and commitment to quality and compliance. **Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)** * Time\-to\-competency for new maintenance hires * Training completion and compliance rates * Effectiveness of training programs (assessment results, performance improvements) * Reduction in maintenance\-related deviations linked to skill gaps * Continuous improvement of training materials and programs **Reporting Structure** * Reports to: Maintenance Manager / Site Training Lead (as applicable) * Key Interfaces: HR, Engineering, Operations, Quality, Training Academy

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Hollyfort, a private company specialising in public works for Government Bodies, including Healthcare and County Councils, is expanding! We're seeking multiple Maintenance Technicians to join our growing team to work on exciting projects throughout Dublin and Leinster. We’re looking for a practical, hands\-on Maintenance Technician who can handle a wide variety of household repair and improvement jobs. If you’re someone who takes pride in fixing things properly and leaving customers happy, this could be the role for you. **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Visiting homes, commercial properties, and public spaces to carry out maintenance and repairs. * Performing a wide range of tasks, including painting, patch repairs, tiling (preferred but not required), and general upkeep. * Undertaking and resolving maintenance problems to a basic level across all disciplines * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance to ensure properties remain in top condition. * Collaborating with tradespeople, general operatives, and subcontractors to complete projects efficiently. * Keeping accurate records of maintenance checks and documenting all work carried out. * Providing a friendly, reliable service that clients and customers can trust. * Proper and safely use of hand and power tools **What We’re Looking For:** * Versatile practical skills across domestic maintenance and repair work. * Experience with painting and decorating, patch repairs, and general upkeep. * Tiling, carpentry, or minor plumbing skills would be a bonus. * A hands\-on, problem\-solving approach and the ability to handle a variety of tasks. * Reliability, good communication, and pride in delivering quality work. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Valid driving license **Why Join Hollyfort?** * Every day is different – no two projects or jobs are the same. * Excellent opportunity to grow and develop * We value skill, initiative, and the satisfaction of a job well done. * You’ll have the chance to use your versatility to make a real difference for our customers. Benefits: * Company Pension * Company mobile phone * Company events * Bike to Work and Tax Saver Scheme * Employee Assistance Program * Learning and Development * Uniform provided Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Experience: * Maintenance Technician: 1 year (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Safe Pass (preferred) * Manual Handling Certificate (preferred) * B Driving Licence (preferred) Work Location: On the road

title: Managing Quantity Surveyor
company: GEDON™ | Commercial & Executive Search for the Built Environment
description: **Company Overview** Our client is one of Ireland's leading Modern Methods of Construction (MMC) specialists, with a strong and growing reputation for delivering complex modular projects across the education, healthcare and defence sectors. Operating on a Design \& Build basis, they bring a fully integrated approach to project delivery — combining in\-house design capability with precision\-engineered modular construction solutions. With a turnover approaching €100m and a well\-secured pipeline, this is a business at an exciting inflection point. They have established themselves as a recognised and respected name in the Irish MMC market and are now actively pursuing expansion into Northern Ireland, creating opportunities for commercially astute professionals who understand both jurisdictions. Despite their scale, this remains a young and agile organisation — fast\-moving, collaborative and free from the bureaucracy that can slow down larger contractors. Decisions are made quickly, talented people are given real responsibility, and the culture is one of continuous improvement and shared ambition. For a driven QS professional, this is an environment where your contribution will be visible and your progression genuinely supported. **Working Environment \& Projects** You will be joining a tight\-knit commercial team at the heart of a business delivering technically complex modular schemes across Ireland. Projects range from purpose\-built modular education facilities and healthcare units to specialist defence structures — each requiring a high level of commercial rigour, programme awareness and design co\-ordination from the outset. The role sits at a senior level within the commercial function and carries real influence. As Managing QS, you will take ownership of post\-contract commercial management while also contributing meaningfully to the estimating and pre\-contract side of the business — giving you visibility across the full project and tender pipeline. This breadth of involvement is one of the defining features of the role and a genuine differentiator for the right candidate. The office is based in Co. Meath, offering a smart alternative to the Dublin commute, with a well\-structured hybrid model in place — typically two to three days working remotely and two to three days on site or in the office. The team operates with a strong sense of trust and autonomy, and this balance reflects the progressive culture the business has worked hard to build. With the business now actively tendering in Northern Ireland, there is a strategic opportunity for the right individual to play a meaningful role in shaping the commercial approach for that expansion — particularly valuable for those with prior experience working under NEC or JCT forms of contract in the NI or UK market. **Responsibilities** * Lead and manage the commercial function across live modular construction projects from contract award through to final account * Prepare, negotiate and agree subcontractor and supplier packages, ensuring best value and contract compliance * Manage cost reporting, cash flow forecasting and CVR preparation, presenting commercial performance to senior leadership * Administer contracts in line with RIAI, PWC or NEC forms as applicable, managing variations, claims and early warnings * Support the estimating department in preparing tender submissions and pricing strategies for new project opportunities * Contribute to the development of the commercial approach for Northern Ireland projects and cross\-border tendering activity * Identify and manage commercial risk throughout the project lifecycle, implementing mitigation strategies as required * Mentor and support junior commercial staff, contributing to team development and knowledge\-sharing * Liaise with clients, designers and project teams to ensure commercial matters are proactively managed and resolved **What You Need** * Minimum 10 years of post\-graduate Quantity Surveying experience, with a strong background in main contracting environments * Degree\-qualified in Quantity Surveying, Commercial Management or a related construction discipline * Demonstrated experience managing the full commercial lifecycle on projects of significant value and complexity * Exposure to the estimating function and an ability to contribute to pre\-contract and tender processes * Experience working with NI or UK clients, contract forms or procurement frameworks is highly desirable given the business's expansion plans * Strong knowledge of RIAI, PWC or NEC forms of contract; experience across multiple forms an advantage * Excellent communication, negotiation and stakeholder management skills, with the confidence to operate at senior level **What You'll Get in Return** This role offers a salary of circa €100k, commensurate with experience, alongside a competitive benefits and pension package. You will join a business at a genuinely exciting stage of growth, with the opportunity to shape the commercial function and play a central role in the company's expansion into new markets. The hybrid working model — two to three days remote — provides genuine flexibility, and the Co. Meath base means you benefit from an easy commute without the daily grind of Dublin traffic. For the right individual, this is a senior platform with real long\-term potential. **How to Apply** To apply for this role, please send an up\-to\-date CV via the Apply Now button. For a confidential discussion, you can also contact **Finn Davey** on **\+353 1 270 9457** or email **finn@gedonexecutive.com** .

title: Microsoft Dynamics Consultant

scalable enterprise solutions across their digital platforms. **Job Responsibilities** * Design and develop solutions within Dynamics 365 Finance \& Operations and associated platforms, ensuring alignment with business and architectural requirements * Collaborate with business stakeholders and functional teams to gather and translate requirements into robust technical designs * Build and customise components using technologies such as C\#, .NET, JavaScript and related frameworks * Develop and manage integrations between D365 and external systems, ensuring reliable and efficient data exchange * Support full lifecycle delivery including design, development, testing, deployment and post\-go\-live support * Produce and maintain clear technical documentation including solution designs and process flows * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives, including development standards and reusable components * Stay up to date with platform updates and recommend enhancements to optimise system performance **Experience Required** * Minimum 5 years’ experience working with Dynamics 365 Finance \& Operations and related technologies * Proven track record delivering end\-to\-end D365 solutions in complex environments * Strong development experience across Microsoft technologies including C\#, .NET and web technologies * Experience integrating enterprise systems using APIs and middleware solutions * Solid understanding of data architecture, security models and system design principles * Ability to communicate technical concepts clearly to non\-technical stakeholders * Experience working independently and managing competing priorities effectively **Desirable Skills** * Experience with Power Platform and Dataverse * Exposure to cloud services such as Azure Functions, Logic Apps or similar tools * Knowledge of modern front\-end frameworks (e.g. React or similar) * Familiarity with DevOps practices and CI/CD pipelines * Experience working within a Centre of Excellence or structured governance environment **Educational Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or a related discipline **Working Hours \& Benefits** * Standard Monday to Friday working week * Flexible and hybrid working options available * Competitive salary package **How to Apply** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail.

title: Mobile QA Architect
description: **About Us:** Gen is a global company dedicated to powering Digital Freedom through its trusted consumer brands including Norton, Avast, LifeLock, MoneyLion and more. Our combined heritage is rooted in financial empowerment and cyber safety for the first digital generations, and today we deliver award\-winning cybersecurity, online privacy, identity protection and financial wellness solutions to nearly 500 million users in more than 150 countries. Together, we share a collective passion and vision to protect consumers and help them grow, manage and secure their digital and financial lives. We’re always looking for smart, fearless and high\-impact talent who see AI as a teammate – leveraging it to move faster and deliver meaningful results. When you’re part of Gen, you’ll have the flexibility, tools and support to do your best work and grow your career – from flexible working options and time off to competitive pay, benefits and well\-being programs. At Gen, we are scrappy and relentlessly customer driven. We create room for healthy debate, experimentation and continuous learning, and we seek out people with different experiences, identities and ideas to join our team. You’ll work with people who back each other, respect each other and understand that our differences are a competitive advantage. If this sounds like you, we’d love you to be part of Gen. **About The Role:** We are pioneering an AI\-first development culture. Every aspect of our product development lifecycle is infused with AI adoption—from ideation and design to coding, testing, and deployment. Our mission is to lead the industry in AI\-powered mobile innovation. Quality assurance represents a critical leverage point for AI\-driven productivity in mobile engineering. In this role, you will help transform QA into a strategic, AI\-enabled function that drives both velocity and quality across the mobile engineering organization. In this role, you will: * Build and mentor a world\-class mobile QA engineering team, ensuring high\-impact Android and iOS applications meet the highest quality standards for millions of users worldwide. * Champion AI\-first testing practices, leveraging tools such as GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, and generative testing frameworks to automate test creation, optimize test coverage, accelerate bug detection, and elevate product quality. * Partner with engineering, product, and design teams to define and drive a comprehensive quality assurance strategy for mobile products. * Lead the evolution of test automation frameworks and infrastructure, leveraging AI to create intelligent, maintainable, and scalable test suites. * Foster a quality\-first mindset across the mobile engineering organization, positioning QA as a strategic partner rather than a bottleneck. * Remove blockers, set clear priorities, and enable your team to deliver high\-quality outcomes in a fast\-paced, global environment. **About You:** * You are an inspiring leader who attracts and develops top talent, leads by example, communicates clearly, and builds trust through authenticity and competence * You have an AI\-native mindset and believe in AI’s transformative potential for quality assurance as a force multiplier, not a replacement * You bring deep technical expertise in mobile QA across Android and/or iOS, including understanding of device fragmentation, OS\-specific challenges, and robust test strategy design * You are a hands\-on manager who stays connected to the work, writes test code, and understands the day\-to\-day realities of your team * You have experience with programming languages such as Java, Kotlin, Swift, or Python * You are curious and continuously learning, especially in AI tooling for QA, mobile testing innovations, and team dynamics * You thrive in cross\-functional collaboration and build strong relationships with product, engineering, design, and DevOps teams * You are a strategic quality builder who shapes team culture, establishes meaningful quality metrics, and drives continuous improvement **What's Next:** If you're excited about this opportunity, we’d love to hear from you! The hiring process consists of two technical interview rounds and one managerial interview. Throughout the process, you will have the opportunity to meet the hiring manager and future team members.

title: Network Service Engineer
description: **Your role** The Network Service engineer is responsible for monitoring and maintaining Digital Realty's Interconnectivity Network infrastructure to ensure minimum network downtime and customer service impacts. Troubleshoot all network incidents and any customer reported services issue within agreed upon SLA. If needed, the engineer is responsible for coordinating with internal and external providers to troubleshoot and fix any network/customer service issues. **What you’ll do** * Real\-Time Monitoring: Proactively tracking network health, traffic, and performance metrics to prevent failures. * Trouble Ticket Support: Create and manage trouble tickets with our equipment vendors and our fiber providers and support customer facing trouble tickets (Cloud Connect, Service Exchange, DRIX, IP Bandwidth, Metro Connect (Dark Fiber), Metro Connect (Ethernet), Metro Connect (Managed Wave), Ports, Links, Connections, Virtual Router). * Incident Management \& Response: Detecting, analyzing, and resolving network outages, outages, or performance degradation. * Maintenance \& Updates: Support the installation, configuring, and updating hardware, and firmware, including patch management. * Reporting: Providing detailed analysis regarding network performance, availability and recommending infrastructure design and routing improvements. * Incident Management, Incident Diagnosis and Incident Resolution within SLA's, OLA's and KPI's, ensuring that all incidents are investigated in a timely manner. * Provide regular updates to ticketing systems while progressing incidents through to resolution. * Liaising and escalating with external suppliers and other Digital Realty departments to ensure minimum disruption and downtime on the network. Staying up to date with new network technology supported by the NOC. * **What you’ll need** * A degree in telecommunications or equivalent, relevant industry experience. * Experience working with Juniper, Ciena, and Cisco network equipment are a plus. * Professional certifications such as CCNA, JNCIA, or Optical Associate/Foundation are a plus. * Proven experience working within a Network Management or Operations Center environment in the Managed Services (Data or Voice), Telecoms, ISP, or Cloud Service Provider sectors. * Experience working within a Data Center (co\-location) environment is advantageous. * Hands\-on experience with network monitoring tools and trouble\-ticketing systems. * Strong understanding of TCP/IP networking fundamentals. * Proficient in LAN and WAN troubleshooting, including physical layer cabling issues in co\-location environments. * Working knowledge of IP/MPLS networks (experience with Juniper MX Series is desirable). * Working experience of Optical Ethernet and Transport Network. (Ciena 6500 and Waveserver AI desirable) * Working experience with Optical Ethernet and Transport Networks (experience with Ciena 6500 and Waveserver AI is desirable). * Solid understanding of routing principles. * Experience working with VMware and Linux\-based systems. * Familiarity with Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute, AWS Direct Connect, Google Cloud Interconnect, or other CSP connectivity services is an advantage. * Scripting knowledge in Python or experience with Ansible automation tools is a plus. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills and strong attention to detail.

title: Operations Associate (Part-Time) - Grafton Street
description: **WHY JOIN ALO?** Mindful movement. It’s at the core of why we do what we do at ALO—it’s our calling. Because mindful movement in the studio leads to better living. Objective The Operations Associate is critical in the store team and is responsible for driving profitability and efficiency. The Operations Associate will work closely with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead in initiating, completing work, and providing feedback to the team to achieve optimal results supporting total store operations. Key Job Responsibilities * Execute inventory management processes effectively with a focus on maximizing inventory accuracy and reducing shrink * Investigate and root cause inventory accuracy issues, partnering with the General Manager to escalate process or system gaps * Support execution of key operational functions including shipment, receiving and processing, reverse transfers, sales floor recovery, intra\-day sales floor replenishment, floor set updates, and supply management * Maintain and champion strong visual standards for the sales floor * Work closely with reporting, budgets, expenses, inventory control, supplies, vendor relationships, technology, and facilities to ensure maintained and maximized * Support a safe work environment and efficient operation through strong stockroom standards and processes * Move dynamically on the retail floor to assess and fulfill the needs of the business, team and guests * Execute company\-level operations\-focused directives, projects and initiates, in partnership with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead * Assists in processing shipment and ensuring product flow; title: Python Developer

Great opportunity and chance to join a growing team working in an exciting industry You must also have completed projects with an AI focus as this role sits in a new AI team they are building **Responsibilities** * Designing, developing, testing, and maintenance of software applications that are reliable, scalable, and secure. * Collaboration with other engineers, product managers, designers, and architects to implement solutions that meet business needs. * Contribute to system design and technical decision\-making in a cloud\-based, serverless environment * Write clean, maintainable, and well\-tested code * Operating to high standards and best practices * Participate in code reviews, pair programming, and knowledge sharing. * Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve software defects and performance issues. * Assist in the continuous improvement of development processes and delivery pipelines. **Required skills and experience** * 3–5 years of professional software development experience. * Programming language of Python, * Strong understanding of object\-oriented design and clean code principles. * Experience with AWS services, especially serverless (e.g., Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, S3\) * Infrastructure\-as\-code tools like Terraform or CloudFormation. * Familiarity with JavaScript/TypeScript and modern front\-end frameworks would be a benefit but not essential * Experience working with NoSQL (e.g., DynamoDB, MongoDB) * Exposure to DevOps practices, including CI/CD pipelines, Git, Docker, * Monitoring and logging tools * A collaborative mindset and eagerness to learn, grow, and be a team member * A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. title: QA Engineer
description: **About Air Apps** At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger \- and moving faster. We’re a family\-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI\-powered Personal \& Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal, in 2018 \- and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco \- we’ve remained self\-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide. Our long\-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI\-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe. Join us on this journey to redefine resource management \- and change lives along the way. ****The Role**** As a **QA Engineer** at, you will play a key role in ensuring the **quality** , **reliability** , and **performance** of our mobile applications. You’ll focus on building r **obust automated tests** and **strengthening API testing processes** while still performing hands\-on functional validation when needed. Working closely with developers, product managers, and designers, you’ll help identify issues early, expand test coverage, and implement scalable testing strategies that guarantee a smooth and stable user experience. Your role will be essential in **detecting bugs, verifying feature implementations, automating tests, and validating software stability before deployment** . * This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast\-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts. **Responsibilities** * Perform manual testing on web and mobile applications to ensure a seamless user experience. * Perform API tests and automation using Postman. * Creating and maintaining Postman documentation. * Create and execute automated regression, functional, performance, and API tests. * Develop and execute detailed test plans, test cases, and test scripts. * Document and report bugs, working closely with developers to resolve issues. * Ensure feature compliance by validating product requirements against test results. * Identify edge cases, inconsistencies, and performance issues. * Work with cross\-functional teams to improve software quality throughout the development lifecycle. * Provide clear and structured feedback to enhance product functionality and user experience. * Maintain test documentation and contribute to quality assurance best practices. **Requirements** * Around 3\+ years of experience in manual and functional testing. * Strong understanding of QA methodologies, testing types, and best practices. * Experience with test case management tools (e.g., TestRail, Zephyr, Xray). * Proficiency in test automation frameworks (e.g., Selenium, Cypress, Appium, Playwright). * Strong experience in programming languages such as Python, Java, JavaScript, or TypeScript. * Familiarity with bug tracking systems (e.g., Jira, Trello, Bugzilla). * Experience in testing web and mobile applications (iOS \& Android). * Strong attention to detail and ability to identify complex usability issues. * Experience with API testing (e.g., Postman). * Ability to work in an agile development environment and adapt to changing priorities. * Strong communication and collaboration skills. ****What benefits are we offering?**** * Apple hardware ecosystem for work. * Annual Bonus * Top\-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind. * Transportation Budget to support your commute needs. * Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well\-being, and more. * Childcare support. * Air Conference \- an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together. * Pension Fund to support your long\-term financial planning. * Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active. * Meals 100% free at the hub. ****Diversity \& Inclusion**** At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger. ****Application Disclaimer**** At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI\-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification. Compensation Range: €44K \- €56K

title: QA Sr Associate
description: **Purpose** The Sr. Associate in Quality Assurance (QA) will be an active member of the QA team and will be assigned to the IQA Quality functional area, which is responsible for the inspection and release of primary packaging components. The Sr. Associate in Quality Assurance will report to a QA Manager and will serve as Quality point of contact for Incoming Inspection of Primary and packaging components. The site is focused on continuous improvement of all work processes and practices, therefore in addition to routine Quality Assurance duties, IQA Snr. Associates may be asked to carry out additional work functions to support site continuous improvement activities. **Responsibilities** \* Testing of primary and secondary components for use in the manufacture of aseptic products. \* Sampling of Excipients and Drug Substance under Grade C conditions. \* Review/approve documentation associated with component testing in preparation for QA Specialist or Qualified Person disposition activities \* Compiling, Review and approval of minor deviation's and CAPAs for closure ensuring compliance with appropriate documentation, whilst ensuring that CAPA actions address root cause and implementation plan dates are achievable \* Participates in customer complaint investigations with suppliers of components. \* Provide training and advice to staff in relation to testing of components and packaging. \* Actively participate in audits and their preparation. \* Review and approve cGMP records (e.g., WO, JP, GDMS, Deviation, CAPA, protocols, reports, SOP, etc.) ensuring compliance with appropriate documentation \* Write, review and approve Standard Operating Procedures in accordance with Company Policies. \* Perform all activities in compliance with Company safety standards and SOPs \* Support continuous improvement and Operational Excellence initiatives \* Any other tasks/projects assigned as per manager's request. **Requirements** \* Must Pass Eye\-Tests required for Visual Inspection \* University degree. Engineering or Science related discipline preferred \* Relevant experience (4 yrs. \+) working in the pharmaceutical or biotechnology industry or other combination of experience and educational background that may otherwise satisfy the requirements of the role. **Preferred Qualifications \& Experience:** \* Excellent written and verbal communication skills \* Experience working with dynamic cross\-functional teams and proven abilities in decision making \* Strong organizational skills, including ability to follow assignments through to completion \* Demonstrated ability in problem solving and experience in managing Root Cause Analysis / Non\-Conformance /Deviation investigations \* Experience working in aseptic operations, protein formulation, vial and syringe filling. **Shift Patterns:** \* 07:00 to 15:00, Monday to Thursday, 07:00 to 14:00 on a Friday \* Late shift is from 14:30 to 22\.30 Monday to Thursday, 13:30 to 20:00 on a Friday.

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: At PJ Hegarty \& Sons, we pride ourselves in a holistic approach to Quantity Surveying. Our responsibility is to manage the company’s financial interests in a manner as wide as is possible and simultaneously assist in project delivery to meet the Client’s vision for the project. These responsibilities include the management of costs and value and also the management of our contractual relationships with the Client and the supply chain. We are currently seeking experienced Quantity Surveyors in the Greater Dublin area. You will be responsible for the cost and value management of projects from mobilisation to the settlement of final accounts. **Overview** As a Quantity Surveyor in our team, you will have progressed from graduate level and will be focused upon becoming a Senior Surveyor. We have a progressive training programme focused upon your development and tailored to meet the requirements of the SCSI/RICS Assessment of Professional Competence. You will be given the tools to assist you manage a trainee or graduate quantity surveyor. You will also participate in departmental training and will be involved in receiving training from seniors and also giving training to juniors. Key Duties * Participate in procurement of specialist trades for the project and make appointment recommendations to the project director. * Assist in the drafting of subcontract Agreements. * Receive, assess, and settle subcontract interim valuations and final accounts. * Review developing design information and advise the wider project team of the cost implications in good time. * Identify and advise the wider project team of value engineering opportunities. * Complete monthly valuations for submission to the Employer’s Representative and agree said valuations. * Participate in the monthly internal cost reporting systems engaging in cost V value reconciliations, turnover reports, prelim cost reports and project outcome projections. * Be a key player in the preparation of the project final account submission to the Client. * Assist in the provision of proactive advice to the project director on matters of contract. * And most importantly Develop and maintain strong working relationships with the wider project team. **Skills/Experience Required** You will have * A level 8 degree in Quantity Surveying or above * 3\+ Years’ experience in a similar role * Good written and oral communication skills with emphasis on ability to provide and explain reports accurately. * A proven track record in dealing with Subcontractors and Clients. * Good problem\-solving skills. * A reasonable knowledge of current forms of Agreement for the industry * You will be\-Computer literate in Microsoft Office, and have some knowledge of Bluebeam, CostX, and Cubit (although training can be given in these) * Able to work well under pressure, on your own and also as part of a team. * Career driven, ambitious and enthusiastic with your eye on promotion to the role of a Senior Surveyor. Further Information At PJ Hegarty and Sons, we acknowledge that your career is a journey and dependent upon that career to date you may be stronger or indeed weaker in some areas. As indicated, we will tailor a training programme to meet any needs and actively encourage Continuous Professional Development. Diversity and Inclusion The Company is an Equal Opportunities employer and recognises its responsibility to provide equality of opportunity to employees and prospective employees. Our Aim is to ensure that no job applicant or employee receives less favourable treatment on the grounds of religious belief, political opinion, gender, marital status, civil status, sexual orientation, disability, race, age and membership of the Traveller community. The Company will not accept or tolerate any form of discrimination, direct or indirect, by any member of the Company. The Company is committed to the fair treatment of employees at all times.

title: Recently Qualified Accountant
description: An opportunity has opened for a Recently Qualified Accountant to join a medium sized accountancy practice based in Dublin. This role offers an excellent opportunity for career progression within a dynamic and growing practice. The successful Qualified Accountant will be expected to work within a diverse team and will help to mentor Junior Accountants. **Responsibilities** * Responsible for range of clients in SME sector with turnover ranging from 1m to 25m turnover. * Oversee and direct junior staff in accounts preparation. * Preparation of Cash Flow Projections and business plans as required by clients. * Dealing with Clients on diverse range of issues in relation to the day to day running of their businesses. **Requirements** * The accountant will be **Fully Qualified ACA/ACCA/CTA** * Excellent communication skills and confident conversing with Clients and stakeholders, through all mediums. * Minimum 2 years’ experience in an Irish practice environment. * Previous tax and accounts preparation experience **Benefits** * **€50,000 \- €65,000 DOE** * Friendly culture. * Clear progression pathway. * Hybrid work model. * Flexible hours. * Paid CPD obligations. * Target based performance bonus. * Free parking * Social events * Wellness initiatives * Early Friday finish during Summer If you are a Recently Qualified Accountant looking for a new opportunity, please contact Andrew Hendrickx for more information. We do not send out your cv without your permission.

title: Receptionist
company: St John of God University Hospital
description: **Receptionist – Permanent, Part\-time** Part\-time – evenings, weekends, bank holidays with flexibility to cover day shifts as and when required **Responsibilities:** * Operation of the hospital’s switchboard with efficiency and courtesy at all times. * Be responsible for all aspects of the work involved in the reception area. * Manage patient, staff, family and visitor interactions with empathy and excellent communication skills * Mange and sort internal/ external post daily. * Communicate relevant information to the wider hospital when appropriate, eg. on call rotas. * Manage the ordering and logging of taxis. * Send SMS reminders to patients for upcoming appointments. * Coordinate visitor sign ins with the security team. * Coordinate walk\-in assessments and admissions with the appropriate staff, ensuring accurate record keeping and electronic records and kept up to date. * Complete complaints / adverse incident reports where necessary on the appropriate IT system. * Update, manage and distribute the hospital’s telephone listings. * Observe the reception area and report entry of any unauthorised persons to the security team and Head of Operations when necessary. * Assist with the issuing and collection of bleeps * Support other staff with the operation of ICT equipment ad software available at reception, eg. fax machine, binding machine, photocopier. * Participate in all staff development and in\-service training relevant to the post as may be organised by the hospital * Provide administrative support for any other team within the administrative department as directed by the Administrative Coordinator or a person designated by him/her. * Be actively involved in future service developments within the hospital. * To be fully familiar with the terms of the Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act and all relevant policy documents of the organisation; i.e. Employee Handbook, Safety Statement, Guidelines for Investigation of Allegations of Non\-Accidental Injury and Safeguarding of Vulnerable Adults and Vulnerable Adolescents. * To be fully familiar with the policies, procedures, protocols and guidelines relevant to the reception area and to be fully compliant with same. * To respect and operate within the framework of the tradition, character and Ethos which govern the work of the Hospital. * To ensure that all departmental reports and Centre records are confidential to the service at Saint John of God Hospital and to maintain confidentiality in respect of matters which come to your knowledge in the course of your official duties. * Demonstrate flexibility in performing your duties and if necessary to work outside the standard working hours should the business need arise. * Assist with data entry projects or administrative work as assigned by the Administrative Coordinator or a person designated by him/her. * Perform any other duties as may be reasonably required. **Qualifications:** * Leaving Certificate or FETAC Level 5 or equivalent required as a minimum * Minimum 2 years’ experience in a similar role * Excellent IT skills \- Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Office Suite * Display excellent customer care skills and empathy

title: Receptionist – Front of House - Dun Laoghaire
description: **Who We Are** Truly Dental is a growing network of modern, state\-of\-the\-art dental practices across Ireland. With a strong focus on patient care, innovation, and community, we create a welcoming environment where both patients and staff feel supported. Our Dunlaoghaire clinic is part of this vision, bringing high\-quality dental services to the local community. **Fast Facts** * 14\+ clinics operating across Ireland * Serving thousands of patients each year with comprehensive dental care * Strong emphasis on career progression for our staff, from entry\-level roles to management positions **Purpose of the Vacancy** We are seeking a **Front of House Receptionist** to join our Dunlaoghaire team. This role is essential to ensuring a smooth patient experience by welcoming visitors, managing appointments, and supporting the clinic's busy operations. **Key Responsibilities** * Greet and welcome patients in a professional and friendly manner * Manage multiline phone system and respond to inquiries * Schedule and confirm appointments efficiently * Prepare patient charts and update records in a timely manner * Handle billing statements and process payments * Support clinic staff with administrative needs * Maintain a welcoming and organized reception area **Requirements: Skills \& Competencies** **Education** * High school diploma or equivalent required * Administrative or medical administration training is a plus **Experience** * Previous front desk or receptionist experience (preferably in a medical or dental environment) * Experience in customerfacing roles with high service standards **Knowledge \& Skills** * Strong command of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook) * Excellent written and verbal communication in English * Ability to multitask in a fastpaced environment * Confident, assertive, and able to handle challenging situations with patients **Core Competencies** * Multitasking and organizational skills * Strong interpersonal communication * Resilience and ability to stand firm with demanding clients * Teamoriented with a proactive attitude **What We Offer** * Competitive hourly rate * Training provided on all internal systems and processes * Clear career progression pathways (Front of House Supervisor, Assistant Practice Manager, Practice Manager) * Supportive, friendly work environment with opportunities to grow within Truly Dental **Key Details before Applying** * Full time role * Schedule includes weekends and evening shifts * Fluent, confident English communication is a must (native or nearnative level)
