title: Tester
description: **Role Overview** We are seeking an experienced Test Analyst to join a delivery team working on a customer\-facing portal and associated CRM backend systems. The successful candidate will work closely with a highly experienced Test Manager, contributing to high\-quality testing across web and CRM platforms within a collaborative and intuitive testing environment. This role suits someone who is comfortable operating independently, can quickly understand requirements, and enjoys working within a structured but supportive test function. **Main Responsibilities** * Design, execute, and document test cases, test scripts, and test scenarios * Perform functional, system, integration, and regression testing * Carry out testing across web\-based applications and CRM systems * Actively participate in defect identification, logging, tracking, and re\-testing * Work closely with developers, business analysts, and the Test Manager to ensure quality outcomes * Contribute to continuous improvement of testing processes and standards * Create and validate test data and data integrity between front end and back\-end systems * Participate in User Acceptance Testing support * Assist in improving QA processes and documentation * Development and refinement of regression test packs * Support testing activities within an Agile / DevOps\-style delivery environment (where applicable) **Key Requirements** * 3\+ years’ experience in a professional Test Analyst / Software Tester role * ISTQB Qualified * Strong experience in web application testing * Proven experience testing CRM platforms * Solid understanding of the software testing lifecycle (STLC) * Experience working from business and technical requirements * Strong attention to detail with excellent communication skills * Excellent organization and time management skills **Nice to Have** * Exposure to Azure DevOps environments and CI/CD pipelines * Experience testing customer or partner\-facing portals * Familiarity with Agile delivery frameworks * Experience testing MS applications and systems (e.g. Power Pages, Power Apps, Dynamics 365 etc.) * Automation testing skills and experience * Experience testing in highly regulated organizations or within QA certified organizations (e.g. ISO 2500\) * Experience of the SDL in a public sector organization

title: Trainee Accountant
description: Job Summary Zurich Life Ireland (ZLAP) is recruiting for a Trainee Accountant to join the financial operations team, part of the Zurich finance team. This role is a controlled function under the CBI Fitness and Probity Standard. Your Role As a Trainee accountant your main responsibilities will include, but not necessarily be limited to, the following: * Daily cash management process \- monitoring \& calculating funding requirements for assigned bank accounts. * Processing of EFT \& cheque payments with responsibility for sourcing the required bank approvers and recording the entries to the relevant cashbooks. * Processing of returned payments, stale cheques \& placing stops on cheque payments. * Allocation of daily EFT receipts to the policyholder administration system, reconciliation of the prior day’s updates \& preparing notifications for large values. * Processing of Direct Debit runs \& returns along with the daily reconciliation of same. * Raising weekly \& monthly cashbook journals to the SAP system. * Responsibility for resolving assigned bank reconciliation differences. * Investigate and reply to any queries assigned to you from the Finops queries mailbox. * Other ad\-hoc duties. Your Skills \& Experience * Excellent accuracy \& attention to detail. * Ability to work in a team environment \& contribute to the performance of the team * Good communicator with excellent interpersonal skills * Well organised \& capable of working to tight deadlines * Ability to think clearly and logically * Intermediate level of problem\-solving skills * Good working knowledge of Microsoft excel \& outlook * Willingness to learn and take ownership of tasks * Interested in completing an accountancy qualification Additional Information Primary work location is Blackrock, Co.Dublin. If you value an exciting and varied working environment and meet the above requirements, then our recruiter is looking forward to receiving your application by clicking on the button “Apply online”. **Who we are** Zurich is one Ireland's leading insurance companies providing a wide range of general insurance and life insurance products and services. The company employs over 1,000 people across its locations in Dublin and Wexford. Zurich is a strong brand, Zurich in Ireland is part of Zurich Insurance Group, a leading multi\-line insurer that serves its customers in global and local markets. Our 55,000 employees worldwide form the basis of our success, helping our customers in 210 countries and territories to understand and protect themselves from risk. In order to deliver our services, we offer a competitive compensation package and interesting opportunities for further training \& development. As a Zurich employee you benefit from a multitude of advantages as well as a strong culture, characterized by acceptance, diversity and team spirit. At Zurich we are an equal opportunity employer. We attract and retain the best qualified individuals available, without regard to race/ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age or disability. This role may be available part\-time or full time. This is because we want the best people for our roles, and we recognise that sometimes those people aren’t available full\-time. Please talk to us at interview about the flexibility you may need.

title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (MEDICINE)
description: **Job Description** **Job Title: UCD BEACON CLINICAL TUTOR (MEDICINE)** **Department: UCD Beacon Hospital Academy \& UCD School of Medicine** **Reports to:** Dr Ruth Pilkington, Director of UCD Beacon Academy, Beacon Hospital **Date: July 2026 (Changeover)** **Available as Full\-time (Tutor \+ Clinical) or Part\-time (Tutor only)** **Overall Purpose of Job** Beacon Hospital has an academic partnership with University College Dublin and one of our principle objectives within this partnership is to support the clinical education programme in the UCD School of Medicine. The clinical tutor role plays an instrumental role in identifying key teaching moments for our undergraduate students who are attached to specialities as part of their experiential learning. All clinical tutors are part of a vibrant community of academic staff who pass on their clinical experience and expertise to the next generation of medical professionals. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables: Clinical Tutor (Medicine)** 1\.1\. Coordinate and participate in the teaching and training of the students in Beacon Hospital. 1\.1\.1\. Provide tutorial support and facilitate shared learning experiences for students while on placements. 1\.1\.2\. Provide lectures as part of the formal clinical curriculum and assist with the preparation of clinical examinations. 1\.1\.3\. Provide a level of supervision of clinical activity appropriate to the competence and experience of the individual student. 1\.1\.4\. Utilise a range of appropriate methods of teaching and assessment to demonstrate clinical learning opportunities \- i.e. OSCE style tutorials, problem\-based learning and bed\-side tutorials. 1\.2\. Liaise with relevant UCD personnel to ensure that clinical placements satisfy academic requirements and ensure integrated delivery of all courses. 1\.3\. Coordinate and partake in the orientation and appraisal of medical students attending Beacon Hospital. 1\.4\. Partake in the competency assessment of interns using the national intern competency framework tools within 30 working days of the intern's commencement date. 1\.5\. Demonstrate leadership by assisting in the professional and personal development of the students and Interns throughout their clinical placement at the Beacon Hospital through promotion of self\-learning, formal lectures and tutorials. 1\.6\. Act as a resource for students and Interns seeking specialty information and guidance. 1\.7\. Ensure that the relevant information about progress and performance is made available to the Beacon Hospital and UCD student placement coordinator. 1\.8\. Act and behave professionally and perform one's duties in a manner that optimises patient care and protects and enhances the reputation of the UCD Beacon Hospital Academy. 1\.9\. Adhere to Hospital Policies and Procedures e.g. JCI standards, Code Blue, Infection Prevention and Control, Falls Reduction and data protection. 1\.10\. Contribute to quality improvement; risk management initiatives and policy development so that the overall clinical education is of the highest quality and reflective of best practice. 1\.11\. Build upon the library of tutorial content available in the hospital. The tutor is required to prepare tutorials that can be used in each of the module s \- i.e. Surgery A/B and Clin Res 1, and that can be reproduced with ease each year by clinical tutors at the Beacon Hospital. These may include high level overviews of certain topics that can be taught to students with supporting artefacts, media, and learning tools. 1\.12\. Engage actively in research and scholarly activity as appropriate to the school or hospital. 1\.13\. Champion the student and Interns' participation in clinical audits to ensure that UCD Beacon Hospital Academy programmes meet quality and patient safety standards and any other applicable performance measures. 1\.14\. Report to the appointed clinical lead consultant within the Beacon in all matters relating to medical students and Interns in the manner requested by said clinical lead. **Person Specification** **Qualifications** Current relevant Full Irish Medical Council Registration. MRCPI/Membership of relevant college is desirable **Experience** Minimum 3 years postgraduate experience in an acute hospital setting. Experience in clinical education of students and junior staff. Experience in the effective management of a poor performing staff member and or student. Attendance at clinical tutor trainee days is required (if not complete the successful candidate will be given the opportunity to attend.) **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** High\-level interpersonal and communication skills (both oral and written) facilitating effective interaction within the work areas, the university and other institutions is a mandatory requirement. Strong organisational skills, ability to prioritise work, to work to deadlines and to use own initiative is a mandatory requirement. Proven experience of working in a quality assurance/improvement environment is a mandatory requirement. Proven ability to work as part of a team is required. Ability to embrace change in a dynamic environment, willingness to adopt new procedures and undertake further appropriate training as required Experience in teaching and examining at third level is desirable. Experience in curriculum development and the development of clinical education programmes is desirable. Evidence of research and peer reviewed publications is desirable. Good knowledge of university rules, regulations and procedures, especially those pertaining to examinations, registration and student welfare is required. Experience in research development at third level is desirable. Experience of general and academic administration is desirable. Display awareness and appreciation of the service users and the ability to empathise with and treat others with dignity and respect. Demonstrate sufficient clinical knowledge and evidence\-based practice to carry the out duties and responsibilities of the role. Demonstrate an ability to apply knowledge to best practice standards. All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**
